job_description:
Business Intelligence & Reporting Analyst

Target Salary: $100,000 (commensurate with experience and skills) + Bonus

Work Location: Hybrid (Denver, CO) | Report to Head of Enterprise Operations

Are you ready to be a part of something BIG and GROUNDBREAKING?

At Covered, we are focused on building the future of insurance. Joining Covered Insurance means stepping into a FAST-PACED, INNOVATIVE environment where you can help to REDEFINE the future of insurance. Unlike traditional firms, we leverage cutting-edge APIs, pre-built UI components, and a full-service licensed stack to create smarter, more customer-centric solutions.

Who Is Covered?

Covered, a best-in-class B2B2C embedded insurance marketplace, intends to make insurance easy to understand and painless to purchase. We believe the independent agent is increasingly important, and technology should be built to empower the agent and deliver delightful digital experiences to the customer. By partnering with lenders, financial institutions, and fintech companies, we deliver our personalized embedded insurance marketplace to their customers.

About This Role

Covered is seeking a Business Intelligence and Reporting Analyst to help transform data into meaningful insights that guide strategic decisions across our fast-paced Insurtech. In this role, you’ll collaborate with teams across operations, insurance, product, marketing, and finance to understand business needs and deliver clear, actionable reporting. You’ll build and maintain dashboards, manage data requests, ensure data accuracy, and provide performance analysis that drives growth and efficiency. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a collaborative environment, enjoys solving complex data challenges, and is passionate about enabling data-driven decision making.

About You And Your Experience
• Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field (e.g., Computer Science, Statistics, Engineering) or equivalent experience.
• 3+ years of professional work experience in similar role(s).
• Experience building visuals and managing datasets in Sigma Computing business intelligence tool, Databricks or others such as Looker, Tableau, Power BI, etc.
• Strong SQL skills.
• Understanding of dimensional modeling/star schemas and KPI design.
• Proven track record of delivering impactful data-driven results and visualizations that impact business goals and decision-making.
• Ability to work in a dynamic environment, and adept at mastering new processes and procedures.
• Strong computer skills and aptitude to quickly learn new systems.
• Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present insights clearly and persuasively.
• Must have a high level of initiative, drive and desire to learn new skills and information.
• Preferred: Hubspot CRM experience.
• Preferred: Dialpad telephony system experience.

You Say Benefits? We Say “ItsCovered”

We believe in taking care of our team with a competitive benefits package that includes:
• Culture: work with passionate, innovative professionals who love what they do. Learn from the best, work with the best!
• Medical, Dental, and Vision Insurance to keep you healthy and covered.
• FSA and HSA for added financial flexibility.
• Life Insurance, AD&D, STD, and LTD to protect you and your loved ones.
• 401(k) Retirement Savings Plan to help you build a secure future.
• Casual Time Off Policy: take the time you need to recharge.
• 13 Paid Holidays per year to celebrate and relax.

job_description:
**Business Intelligence Analyst**
• *ADP is hiring a Business Intelligence Analyst for the HR Outsourcing (HRO) Analytics** organization. This role is accountable for supporting leaders to make more data driven decisions, and creating business intelligence tools, visualizations, standards, and insights presentations. This is a highly collaborative position that works closely with senior leaders, key stakeholders, and junior team members. The BI Analyst will report to the Data Analytics Director and has no direct reports.
• *Key Responsibilities:**

+ Develop BI visualizations and actionable insights presentations based on business priorities

+ Interact with key stakeholders to understand requirements and business information

+ Coach business partners on how to utilize dashboards and focus on continuous improvement to drive adoption

+ Adhere to BI standards of excellence to establish the trusted HRO Analytics brand

+ Coach and support junior team members towards excellence in BI best practices

+ Excellent written and verbal communication and story-telling skills with ability to present simply to non-technical audiences in a simple and easy to understand way

+ Manage priorities independently from start to finish

+ Feel very comfortable operating in ambiguity in a fast-paced, fail-fast environment

+ Strong analytical mindset where details matter and the big picture is the guide
• *Required Qualifications:**

+ 3 years experience in Business Intelligence/Analytics

+ Bachelor's Degree in Business, Finance, Tech or related field, or equivalent in education and experience

+ Strong data presentation and visualization skills; experience and formal training with data visualization (e.g., advanced proficiency in Tableau or Power BI)

+ Strong analysis and pattern recognition skills, as well as synthesis skills, to be able to streamline work

+ Strong expertise with business strategy and supporting organizations with taking action on insights

+ Advanced skills creating insights from data and automating data processes

+ Advanced proficiency in MS PowerPoint to create compelling stories through insights

+ Intermediate competency in executing ETL process with little to no supervision using a combination of SQL, Python, and/or Alteryx

+ Experience with relational databases
• *Preferred Qualifications:**

+ MBA or Advanced degree in analytics or similar field

+ Advanced experience with Microsoft Excel **highly preferred**

+ Experience with customer retention strategies **highly preferred**

+ Revenue management experience **highly preferred**

+ Consulting experience from MBB/Big 4 or Fortune 500 experience

+ Change management and large-scale transformation experience

+ Strong SQL and/or Python skills are a plus

+ Advanced competency in SQL with ability to perform joins, unions, use CTEs, and execute complex subqueries

job_description:
AXS connects fans with the artists and teams they love. Each year we sell millions of tickets to thousands of incredible events – from concerts and festivals to sports and theater – at some of the most iconic venues in the world. Since our founding in 2011, we've consistently pushed the industry forward and improved experiences for fans, making it easier than ever to discover events, find the perfect seats, and enjoy unforgettable live entertainment, and we continue to lead the evolution of our industry today.

The Role

The Sr Business Intelligence Analyst will contribute to a growing team as we scale those offerings and continue to build meaningful data insights for the business. This role will work directly with AXS data leadership as well as clients and partners in the US and abroad (Europe and Japan).

What Will You Do?
• Leverage strong analysis competencies to drive business insights across AXS through SQL, Looker and Google & Adobe Analytics.
• Develop ongoing performance reporting for management in all operational areas of the business and distribute reports as needed. Provide in-depth analysis of web analytics & marketing data and summarize findings with recommendations.
• Mentor junior team members as it relates to analysis best practices.
• Contribute strong analyses to the creation of business insights & recap presentations.
• Monitor data sources & ensure the accuracy of data for reporting purposes.
• Drive complex problems through to solutions.
• Work across functions in the organization and with clients and partners to understand and address their data & analysis needs.
• Build LookML models & Looker dashboards to communicate insights.

What Will You Bring?
• BA/BS Degree (4-year) (Advanced Degree Preferred) in Business, Analytics, MIS, Economics or another analytical field.
• 4-6 years in Business Analytics, MIS, Economics or another analytical field.
• Experience with digital and/or social media marketing & web analytics.
• Experience with modern analytic & data visualization tools.
• Experience with programming and/or statistical analysis tools (e.g., SQL, R, Python, SAS).
• Strong quantitative analytical skills, with the ability to derive insights from large data sets, exceptional attention to detail, and a desire to creatively solve challenging problems.
• Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to clearly present complex concepts and analytical findings to a wide variety of audiences, from executives to field staff.
• General business acumen, with the ability to analyze the live events industry and recommend business improvements.
• Ability and initiative to manage multiple short and long-term projects independently, while building effective partnerships with stakeholders across the company.
• Comfort with the Microsoft Office suite as well as advanced analytical tools & data analysis methodologies.
• Willingness to adapt to a fast-paced work environment; hack, test and learn mentality; team player.

Bonus points if You Have
• Music or other entertainment experience a plus; a passion for music and knowledge of artists and industry trends preferred.
• Certified Business Intelligence Professional.

Pay Scale

$78,000- $90,000

Bonus

This position is eligible for a bonus under the current bonus plan requirements.

Benefits

We offer a comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental and vision insurance, paid holidays, vacation and sick time, company paid basic life insurance, voluntary life insurance, parental leave, 401k Plan (with a current employer match of 3%), flexible spending and health savings account options, and wellness offerings. Currently, full time employees are eligible for these benefits on the first day of employment.
• Employer does not offer work visa sponsorship for this position.

What's in it for You?
• Extraordinary People – we're not kidding!
• Meaningful Mission – Helping revolutionize an industry and deliver better experiences for fans and clients around the world.
• Development and Learning – Opportunities for learning and leveling up through training and education reimbursement.
• Community & Belonging – A range of Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) that foster connection, inclusion, and professional growth. Access to meaningful volunteer opportunities and community engagement programs to make a positive impact beyond the workplace.

Interview Process
• Stage 1: 20-30 min virtual interview (Recruiter)
• Stage 2: 30-min virtual interview (hiring manager)
• Stage 3: 1 hour team interview- case study presentation
• Stage 4: 30 minute executive interviews
• This schedule may be subject to change.

More about AXS

AXS, a subsidiary of AEG, sells millions of tickets each year for 1600+ premier venues, sports teams, and event organizers across North America, Europe, Asia, Australia and New Zealand. Headquartered in Downtown Los Angeles, California, AXS employs more than 900 professionals in multiple locations worldwide. In each location you'll find a team of dedicated, diverse employees (we've dubbed ourselves "Fanatix") who create groundbreaking products and services in a fun, fast-paced environment.

More about AEG

For more than 20 years, AEG has played a pivotal role in transforming sports and live entertainment. Annually, we host more than 160 million guests, promote more than 10,000 shows and present more than 22,000 events across the globe. We are committed to innovation, artistry, and community, leveraging the power of our 300+ venues, leading sports franchises, marquee music brands, integrated entertainment districts, premier ticketing platform and global sponsorship activations to create memorable moments that give the world reason to cheer.

Our business is interwoven with the human mind and heart, and we strive to build a diverse and inclusive company that reflects the artists, athletes, and fans that we host; reach beyond traditional boundaries to support the communities in which we operate; and minimize our impact on the environment by adopting sustainable practices throughout our business operations.

We are dedicated to a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace, so if you're excited about this role but can't "check every box" in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be the right candidate for this or other roles.

AEG reserves the right to change or modify the employee's job description whether orally or in writing, at any time during the employment relationship. AEG may require an employee to perform duties outside their normal description.

AEG's policy is to hire the most qualified applicants, and we comply with all applicable federal, state and local employment laws in making hiring and employee decisions. We are an equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate against applicants or employees on the basis of race, color, marital status, disability, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, national origin, genetic information, veteran status, or any other legally protected status recognized by applicable federal, state or local law.

job_description:
GENERAL DESCRIPTION OF POSITION

The Senior Business Intelligence Analyst is responsible for designing and developing complex SQL using various tools such as Microsoft Report Builder, SSMS, and PowerBi.

ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

1. Advanced proficiency in SQL for writing complex queries and understanding database schemas. This duty is performed daily.

2. Develops and deploys new reports and various visual representations including self-service reports using RPB, Power BI, and other BI Tools to meet the reporting needs of the organization. This duty is performed daily.

3. Develop SQL scripts, SQL stored procedures and SQL Views for various reporting and data development projects. This duty is performed weekly.

4. Collaborate with various teams (such as marketing, finance, operations) to understand their data needs. This duty is performed weekly.

5. In-depth understanding and application of the BI reporting life cycle, including Requirements Gathering, Analysis, Design and Development, Testing, Work Estimation and Supporting Documentation. This duty is performed weekly.

6. High level of accountability and a dedication to producing high-quality work that is easily consumable by end users. This duty is performed daily.

7. Excellent time management and prioritization skills; ability to manage multiple projects and priorities and meet established timelines. This duty is performed daily.

8. Critical thinking, analytical, logical problem solving, organizational, and presentation skills. This duty is performed weekly.

9. General understanding of SSIS packages. This duty is performed monthly.

10. Conduct reviews of peer data and reporting development to ensure solutions meet regulation standards, are built for performance, and meet development best practices. This duty is performed monthly.

11. Solve complex data issue using data analyst skills to determine root cause problem for data integrity and data quality issues. This duty is performed weekly.

12. The ability to work in a constant state of alertness and in a safe manner. This duty is performed daily.

13. Complete required BSA/AML training and other compliance training as assigned. This duty is performed quarterly.

14. Perform any other related duties as required or assigned.

QUALIFICATIONS

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty mentioned satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required.

EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE

Technical degree required in such disciplines as Computer Engineering, CPA, etc., plus 4 years related experience and/or training, and 3 years related management experience, or equivalent combination of education and experience.

COMMUNICATION SKILLS

Ability to read, analyze, and understand common scientific and technical journals, financial reports, and legal documents; ability to respond to complex or difficult inquiries or complaints from customers, regulatory agencies, or members of the business community.

MATHEMATICAL SKILLS

Ability to work with mathematical concepts such as probability and statistical inference, and fundamentals of plane, algebra, solid geometry and trigonometry.

CRITICAL THINKING SKILLS

Ability to define problems, collect data, establish facts, and draw valid conclusions. Ability to interpret an extensive variety of technical instructions in mathematical or diagram form and deal with several abstract and concrete variables.

REQUIRED CERTIFICATES, LICENSES, REGISTRATIONS

Not indicated.

PREFERRED CERTIFICATES, LICENSES, REGISTRATIONS

Licenses/Certifications: Technical certifications in relevant application(s) strongly preferred (i.e., PowerBi). Associate Certified Analytics Professional (aCAP, Comptia Data+) or another vendor-neutral analytics certification preferred.

SOFTWARE SKILLS REQUIRED

Advanced: 10-Key, Database

Intermediate: Accounting, Alphanumeric Data Entry, Presentation/PowerPoint, Programming Languages, Spreadsheet, Word Processing/Typing

RESPONSIBILITY FOR WORK OF OTHERS

Carries out supervisory responsibilities in accordance with the organization's policies and applicable laws. Responsibilities may include but not limited to interviewing, hiring and training employees; planning, assigning and directing work; appraising performance, rewarding and disciplining employees; addressing complaints and resolving problems.

Supervises a small group (1-3) of employees in the same or lower classification. Assigns and checks work; assists and instructs as required, but performs same work as those supervised, or closely related work, most of the time. Content of the work supervised is of a non-technical nature and does not vary in complexity to any great degree.

WORKING CONDITIONS

Normal working conditions as found within an office setting, wherein there is controlled temperature and a low noise level, plus a minimum of distractions.

ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS

The following work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

The noise level in the work environment is usually moderate.

PHYSICAL ACTIVITIES

The following physical activities described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions and expectations.

Semi-repetitive, low physical. Semi-repetitive type work which requires periods of concentration for varied time cycles as prescribed by the tasks.

While performing the functions of this job, the employee is continuously required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel, talk or hear; regularly required to sit, reach with hands and arms; occasionally required to walk. The employee must occasionally lift and/or move up to 10 pounds. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision.

ADDITIONAL INFORMATION

-Experience must include development of BI solutions (metadata, reporting, analytics, dashboards, report definitions, data extracts, etc.) (Financial sector preferred)

-Advanced level of executing, implementing and supporting BI solutions based upon business requirement, content knowledge and multiple data source types

-Experience must include knowledge of SQL (designer, queries)

employer_name: Great Day Improvements: A Family of Brands
job_description:
About the position

Since its founding 13 years ago, Great Day Improvements, LLC has grown rapidly toward its vision of becoming one of the largest home improvement companies in the U.S. Headquartered in Twinsburg, Ohio, Great Day Improvements is a \$1.5 billion, vertically integrated, direct-to-consumer provider of premium home improvement products.
The company’s family of brands includes Patio Enclosures®, Champion Windows and Home Exteriors®, Universal Windows Direct®, Apex Energy Solutions®, Stanek Windows®, Hartshorn Custom Contracting, Your Home Improvement Company, K Designers, Leafguard®, Englert®, and The Bath Authority.
With an expanding workforce of over 4,800 employees across 130 metropolitan markets throughout the U.S. and Canada, Great Day Improvements continues to rank among the top home improvement companies nationwide and is one of the fastest growing private companies in America.
Job Summary
The Business Intelligence (BI) Manager leads the BI team and drives data-informed decision making across Great Day’s family of brands. This role oversees a team of data analysts responsible for maintaining and evolving the Tableau Cloud and Power BI environments, ensuring consistent, accurate, and actionable insights are delivered to business stakeholders.
The ideal candidate will combine strong technical understanding of BI platforms with exceptional leadership, communication, and project management skills. This role partners closely with executives, department heads, and cross-functional teams to align BI initiatives with strategic business goals and deliver high-impact analytics solutions.
Location: Twinsburg, OH (Hybrid)
Pay: \$150,000 per year

Responsibilities
• Manage, mentor, and develop a team of data analysts supporting multiple business units and BI platforms
• Foster a collaborative, growth-oriented environment focused on excellence, learning, and innovation
• Set priorities, assign projects, and monitor performance to ensure quality and timely delivery
• Oversee governance, performance, and user adoption of both Tableau Cloud and Power BI environments
• Establish and enforce best practices for dashboard design, data visualization standards, and data governance
• Partner with IT and Data Engineering to ensure data pipelines and sources are accurate, performant, and secure
• Serve as the primary liaison between business units and the BI team to translate business needs into technical solutions
• Lead regular stakeholder meetings to prioritize requests, align reporting goals, and manage competing demands
• Communicate complex data insights and project updates clearly and persuasively to both technical and non-technical audiences
• Manage multiple concurrent BI initiatives, ensuring scope, timelines, and resources are well-balanced
• Champion data literacy initiatives to empower business users to explore and interpret their own data responsibly
• Coordinate with cross-functional teams (Data Science, Data Engineering, Marketing, Operations, Finance) to support enterprise-wide analytics goals
• Support governance frameworks around metadata, data definitions, and report certification
• Collaborate with data engineering to ensure data quality standards are upheld across the analytics ecosystem

Requirements
• Bachelor’s degree in Business Analytics, Data Science, Information Systems, or a related field
• 5+ years of experience in Business Intelligence, Data Analytics, or related roles
• 2+ years of experience in people management or team leadership
• Hands-on experience with Tableau and/or Power BI, including administration, governance, and dashboard development
• Strong understanding of data modeling concepts and SQL
• Proven track record of managing complex analytics projects and balancing multiple stakeholder priorities
• Excellent interpersonal, presentation, and communication skills

Nice-to-haves
• Experience managing BI environments across multiple brands or business units
• Familiarity with Azure data technologies (e.g., Azure Data Lake, Databricks, etc.)
• Experience working in a manufacturing or direct-to-consumer industry
• Experience establishing BI governance, standards, and training programs

job_description: Job Title Sr. BI Analyst Job Description Summary The Business Intelligence team (BI) is responsible for delivery of all data analytics and governance in support of both GOS service lines and GOS clients. The On-Account BI Analyst’s responsibilities include Dashboard design and development, custom analytics and Triana product support. Some of the tasks include: POC dashboard development, reporting support, opportunity assessments, tabular model support, workplace trend analysis and benchmarking analysis. Job Description Responsibilities: Work effectively across all account service lines in coordination with account director and BIAS team to identify client reporting requirements and determine best-fit solutions to resolve reporting needs Identify opportunities for process improvements on account and implement solutions to close process gaps across all service lines to increase efficiency and efficacy of account team by working closely with service line leads and AD Implement short term BI solutions to assist with account team reporting needs without impacting the delivery of the long-term roadmap and final solution Utilize advanced analytics tools to transform data, automate workflows and develop analytical tools Lead data and reporting governance on the account to ensure analytics platform operates as designed Perform data assessment and data mapping exercises to support system integration projects Develop the client specific business glossary, data dictionary, technical documentation and process documentation as required for activities within the data workstream Provide guidance and training to the account team employees on dashboarding platform, Triana and other analytical tools used on account when applicable. Execute user acceptance testing of feature enhancements in both new releases and new dashboards. Troubleshoot Triana bugs and issues with client specific instance of Triana Defining the business requirements from the account and client teams for custom reporting and act as the VOC for Triana platform enhancements Proactively analyse the client’s portfolio and provide support in identifying risks and opportunities. Support data assessment exercise with account team and client while onboarding onto Triana or in preparation for custom reporting solution Assist with account specific data analysis to ensure consistency in reporting and dashboard requirements across the account and the Triana platform. Partner with the development team to expand the Triana offering through dashboard POCs, tabular model measure development and product feature enhancements Provide both verbal and written updates to management on the progress of work.  Liaise with service line leadership to extract data from point applications for uploading into Triana. Monitor KPI performance and report results regularly to the Senior Leadership Team. Review data to be utilized for QBR documents and manage internal associated drafting and review meetings as needed. Description:

The Business Analyst plays a critical role in supporting reporting and dashboard development that enables leadership decision-making, by providing clear, accurate, and timely visibility into established KPIs and performance drivers across the Salience organization. This role partners closely with leaders, clinical teams, and technology partners to translate known reporting needs into structured dashboards, Excel-based reports, and analyses that support operational review, financial tracking, and performance discussions.

The ideal candidate thrives in a mission-driven, fast-paced healthcare environment and is comfortable working within established data models while helping improve reporting consistency and usability over time. This person must be able to prioritize competing reporting requests, communicate clearly with stakeholders, and deliver reliable outputs in a collaborative, business-facing role.

Key Responsibilities

1. Data, Reporting, Performance Analytics & KPIs
• Build and maintain recurring and ad hoc reports and dashboards tracking key metrics such as growth, throughput, operational effectiveness, access rates, and care outcomes.
• Extract and analyze data from Athena, Salesforce, Snowflake, Tableau, and other integrated systems to support accurate and consistent reporting for business and clinical users.
• Present findings in clear, narrative-driven visuals that help leaders understand performance trends and operational drivers.
• Support leadership and functional teams by translating reporting needs into dashboards and metrics aligned with the Salience Standards.
• Model and reinforce Salience’s Ownership Culture by using data to inform discussions and continuous improvement, escalating data quality or reporting gaps as needed.
• Analyze and summarize changes in key metrics by identifying contributing factors and variances across known data sources, supporting leadership discussions on performance without independently determining strategic actions.

2. Systems & Technology Integration
• Support EHR, CRM, and HR system initiatives (Athena, Salesforce, Paylocity, etc.) by providing reporting requirements, validation, and user-focused input.
• Maintain and improve reporting and dashboard standards to ensure consistency, ease of access, and efficient turnaround of reporting requests.

3. Strategic Growth & Business Case Support
• Prepare and maintain historical and current performance data used in growth-related discussions, including new clinics, providers, services, and operational initiatives.
• Support finance, operations, and growth leaders by refreshing and validating reporting inputs used in business cases, capacity planning, and operational reviews.
• Provide structured data outputs and summary reporting to support ROI discussions, while relying on leadership direction for interpretation, recommendations, and decision-making.

4. Financial Modeling & Forecasting Support
• Maintain and update Excel-based financial reporting templates and supporting schedules using data from established systems and approved data sources.
• Prepare recurring monthly, quarterly, and annual reporting updates tied to financial performance, staffing, and operational metrics.
• Support finance-led forecasting and planning processes by providing timely accurate data inputs and variance summaries, without owning forecast assumptions or financial outcomes.

Qualifications

Education & Experience
• Bachelor’s degree in business, Data Analytics, Healthcare Administration, or a related business or analytics field (business-focused degree preferred).
• 2–5 years of experience in business analysis, reporting, or healthcare-related analytics.
• 2+ years in healthcare industry experience is required
• Experience working with healthcare data and business performance reporting is required.

Skills & Competencies
• Advanced proficiency in Excel for structured reporting, financial schedules, and recurring performance updates.
• Strong proficiency in Tableau or a comparable business intelligence platform (e.g., Power BI, Qlik, Looker, MicroStrategy), with the ability to adapt to Salience’s reporting standards.
• Familiarity with Salesforce reporting and dashboards; experience partnering with Salesforce administrators is a plus.
• Exposure to Athena EHR, SQL, and cloud data environments (e.g., Snowflake) is beneficial.
• Strong communication skills — able to translate reporting outputs into clear explanations for clinical and non-clinical audiences.
• Organized, detail-oriented, and comfortable managing multiple reporting requests and shifting priorities.
• Ability to interpret KPI movement and variance across known data sources and clearly explain contributing factors to business and clinical stakeholders.

Performance Metrics
• Leadership has timely and reliable visibility into key operational and performance trends.
• Reports and dashboards are accurate, consistent, and easy for leaders to interpret.
• Reporting requests are delivered within agreed timelines, with clear communication on priorities and turnaround times.
• Reduction in duplicate or outdated reminders and dashboards over time through improved structure and standards.
• Positive feedback from leaders and stakeholders on clarity, usefulness, and responsiveness of reporting support.
• Leadership reports increased clarity and confidence in understanding KPI drivers and performance changes through consistent, well-structured reporting outputs. comfortable managing multiple reporting requests and shifting priorities.

Requirements:

Education & Experience
• Bachelor’s degree in business, Data Analytics, Healthcare Administration, or a related business or analytics field (business-focused degree preferred).
• 2–5 years of experience in business analysis, reporting, or healthcare-related analytics.
• 2+ years in healthcare industry experience is required
• Experience working with healthcare data and business performance reporting is required.

Skills & Competencies
• Advanced proficiency in Excel for structured reporting, financial schedules, and recurring performance updates.
• Strong proficiency in Tableau or a comparable business intelligence platform (e.g., Power BI, Qlik, Looker, MicroStrategy), with the ability to adapt to Salience’s reporting standards.
• Familiarity with Salesforce reporting and dashboards; experience partnering with Salesforce administrators is a plus.
• Exposure to Athena EHR, SQL, and cloud data environments (e.g., Snowflake) is beneficial.
• Strong communication skills — able to translate reporting outputs into clear explanations for clinical and non-clinical audiences.
• Organized, detail-oriented, and comfortable managing multiple reporting requests and shifting priorities.
• Ability to interpret KPI movement and variance across known data sources and clearly explain contributing factors to business and clinical stakeholders.

Location Requirements
• Candidates must currently reside in the Dallas-Fort Worth (DFW) metroplex or within the state of Texas.

Benefits:
• 401(k)
• Dental insurance
• Health insurance

Work Location: In person

job_title: Process Improvement and Business Intelligence Analyst
job_description:
Job Description
Job Description

Company Overview

LP Analyst is the leading cloud-based private asset portfolio management analytics platform for institutional limited partners. We work closely with many of the industry’s most prominent institutional investors and fund managers to get better, more actionable decision-making intelligence into the hands of today’s demanding investors. Our firm sits at the intersection of private asset analytics and consulting and takes both a highly quantitative and qualitative approach to developing its cutting-edge solutions.

If you are a high achiever with a passion for learning about the private asset analytics and consulting landscape while also being part of a strong, motivated and innovative team of forward-thinking professionals, LP Analyst might be the right place for you.

LP Analyst encourages and promotes diversity within its team. Women, minorities, people with disabilities, veterans and members of all underrepresented groups are strongly encouraged to apply.

Job Summary:

LP Analyst is seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented individual to join our team as a Process Improvement and Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst. This person is responsible for enhancing organizational efficiency and decision-making through the implementation of process improvement strategies and the development and maintenance of business intelligence dashboards. In this multi-faceted position, you will collaborate with various teams to support existing process automations and develop new process automations using Remote Process Automation and leveraging AI. You will also support our team in designing, developing and maintaining BI dashboards including integrating data from various sources.

Key Responsibilities:
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather requirements, analyze processes, and propose automation solutions to support the business
• Assist in the development of a prioritized plan for implementing process improvements, considering the potential impact on the organization
• ​​​​​​​Support the implementation of approved process improvements, working closely with project leads and stakeholders
• ​​​​​​​Assist in making necessary adjustments and refinements to implemented processes and maintaining technical documentation accordingly
• Oversee existing automation processes and related systems
• Create and maintain business intelligence reports and visualizations for internal and client-facing platforms
• Integrate data from various sources into cloud infrastructure for use in BI platforms
• Research and evaluate new functionality for internal and client-facing BI platforms

Qualifications and Skills:
• Bachelor's degree in a relevant field, such as Computer Science, Business Administration, or a related discipline
• ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​Expertise in business intelligence tools, particularly Power BI, and proficiency in DAX for advanced calculations
• ​​​​​​​Experience with Microsoft products including Azure, Power Automate, Power Platform and Dynamics
• ​​​​​​​Strong understanding of SQL databases and the ability to translate business requirements into technical specifications
• ​​​​​​​Proven experience in project coordination, product management, or similar roles
• ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​Exceptional communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to convey technical information to non-technical stakeholders
• ​​​​​​​High self-motivation with ability to learn quickly, meet deadlines and seek additional leadership roles
• ​​​​​​​A keen eye for process optimization and automation opportunities, with a willingness to learn and support ongoing initiatives
• ​​​​​​​Detail-oriented, organized, and capable of managing multiple tasks and priorities effectively
• ​​​​​​​Strong problem-solving skills and a proactive mindset
• ​​​​​​​Applicants must be authorized to work for ANY employer in the U.S. We are unable to sponsor or take over sponsorship of an employment Visa for this position.

Why LP Analyst?
• Professional development programs and regular teach-ins on business and industry topics and themes
• Company-sponsored 401k plan, healthcare and dental insurance plans
• Paid time-off that increases with tenure at the firm, plus an annual volunteer day of your choice
• Friendly and collaborative company culture with frequent company-sponsored team events and happy hours and casual dress code
• Great office located in the arts district downtown with paid parking, free snacks and many other office perks
For further information about our team and career opportunities, please visit Private Equity Careers — LP Analyst | Private Asset Portfolio Monitoring Solutions & Analytics Software for Limited Partners

Are you a force for good?Do you enjoyproviding insights through data and excel at presenting complex information to others that is both understandable and action-oriented? Does the opportunity to devote your talents to a cause much greater than yourself excite you? The Arbor Day Foundation is a place you can do work that matters, with people who care. As we say at the Foundation: life is short, love where you work.

As one of the world's largest conservation organizations, the Arbor Day Foundation inspires people to plant, nurture, and celebrate trees. We help others understand and use trees as a solution to global issues (like air quality, water quality, climate change, deforestation, poverty, and hunger) that are critical to survival.

We need a Business Intelligence Analyst toprovide data support to business leaders through dashboards, automated and custom models and ad hoc reports; helping them understand their operational drivers and make sound business decisions.
• **YOU HAVE:***

+Ananalyticalmindset.Youunderstand the cause-effect relationships between drivers and outcomes.You areableto see the problem or opportunity and develop analyses that helps business owners make the best decisions.

+ Technicalskills.You'restronginSQL, data modeling and reporting. Ideally, you have prior experience with Power BI orTableau.

+ Great communication skills.Seeing things from someone else's perspective is how you operate. You enjoy interacting with others and having variety in your work.

+An organizedapproach.You've found success bystayingorganized, adjustingpriorities and communicatingwith stakeholders in a constantly changing business landscape.Youconsidertheimpact of work on other areas when designing solutions.

+ An autonomous nature.Working independently to develop solutions with little explicit directionis your sweet spot.

+ Experience.Our ideal candidate has2-4 years of business intelligence/data analysis experience.
• **YOU WILL:***

+Developframework for how to deliver insights.Along with the BI team, you will play an important role in establishing best practices for how to work with internal areas to provide value through the use of data.

+Buildand maintainreporting solutions in Power BI, SSRS with automation and long-term sustainability in mind.

+ AnswerBI questionsand anticipate needs to support areas with insights into their data.

+Be a partner. You'll work withbusiness stakeholdersto influence decisionsand provide strategic value.

+ Collaborateand learn.You'll take the time to understand and learn processes and the most important questions across multiple areas. This will help youdetermine useful business questions or opportunities and define and design reporting and analytics solutions.
• **LOVE WHERE YOU WORK:***

+We're in this together.A culture focused on a genuine interest in the wellbeing of others, collaboration, and the intrinsic shared purpose to do what's right. We pride ourselves on the culture we've built. We love it, and we know you will too.

+We care about your health.We offer competitive healthcare (health, dental, vision) coverage for you and your loved ones matched with a wellness incentives program.

+We invest in your future. Company paid short-term and long-term disability, life insurance and 401k with company match are available.

+We want you to unplug when needed.We believe in taking your time off without guilt, including the week between Christmas and New Years that we close our Lincoln Campus to recharge.

+We know the little things matter.Team members receive a monthly transportation stipend, a fully stocked kitchen, and access to a plethora of learning and development opportunities.

+We'll round out the experience.We put our culture first, and we host events and experiences throughout the year to show that we care about you (and your family).

job_description:
Datavant is the data collaboration platform trusted for healthcare. Guided by our mission to make the world’s health data secure, accessible and actionable, we provide critical data solutions for organizations across the healthcare ecosystem - including providers, health plans, researchers, and life sciences companies. From fulfilling a single patient’s request for their medical records to powering the AI revolution in healthcare, Datavanters are building the future of how data is connected and used to improve health.

By joining Datavant today, you’re stepping onto a driven and highly collaborative team that is passionate about creating transformative change in healthcare.

At Datavant, our mission is to protect, connect, and deliver the world’s health data to power better decisions and advance human health. As part of our internal team, you help us achieve this mission every day.

By continuing your career at Datavant, you’ll drive transformative change in healthcare alongside a collaborative team.

The Business Intelligence & Analytics function within Datavant Product is actively seeking a detail-oriented and impact-driven data analyst to strengthen our Corporate Cross-Functional Reporting team. This pillar focuses on building a scalable, trusted reporting platform that delivers consistent, transparent, and actionable insights across our finance, accounting, compliance, and people functions. In this role you will help mature our analytics portfolio, drive BI modernization, and enable operational visibility through data. If you are an analyst who thrives at the intersection of data and business, and enjoys collaborating with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, we would love to hear from you!

You Will:
• Partner & Analyze
• Partner with cross-functional stakeholders to understand business needs, scope requirements, and define analytical approaches that inform decision-making.
• Conduct exploratory and targeted analyses using analytical methods and business context to generate actionable recommendations.
• Design and develop business intelligence solutions, including dashboards, reports, and tools that support operational visibility and strategic insights.
• Deliver Scalable BI Solutions
• Lead the development and maintenance of reusable data models and visualizations, ensuring accuracy, scalability, and performance across internal and external reporting at Datavant.
• Collaborate with data engineering and BI teams to ensure data quality, accessibility, and alignment between source systems and reporting layers.
• Enable Continuous Improvement
• Contribute to the automation of recurring analytical workflows, reducing manual reporting and improving turnaround time for business users.
• Drive the growth of team practices and analytical maturity by mentoring peers, documenting solutions, and leading process improvements.
• Stay current on industry best practices in data visualization, BI tools, and analytics methods to continuously elevate team capabilities.

What You Will Bring to the Table:
• Technical Skills
• Proficiency in statistical analysis and programming languages such as SQL and Python for data analysis and modeling.
• Skill in designing clear, insightful visualizations tailored to varied audiences using tools such as Sigma, Power BI, or Tableau.
• Business Acumen & Communication
• Demonstrated ability to craft compelling, data-driven stories that inform strategy and influence decision-making.
• Excellent communication skills with the ability to distill technical concepts for non-technical audiences.
• Proven ability to manage time effectively and prioritize work to meet tight client deadlines.
• Mindset & Experience
• Exceptional initiative and ability to solve problems independently, seek help when needed, and take ownership when navigating ambiguity.
• Experience navigating change with cross-functional collaboration, customer focus, ownership, and a bias for action.
• 4+ years of relevant experience in data analytics or a related field.

Bonus Points If You Have:
• Experience working in healthcare, life sciences, or high-growth environments, especially in domains related to release of information (ROI) or data privacy compliance.
• Understanding of data privacy and security best practices, with awareness of key regulations (e.g., HIPAA).
• Experience working with cloud-based storage and computing platforms (e.g., AWS, GCP, Azure).
• Experience using dbt to build and maintain data models within a modern analytics stack.
• Proven ability to bring structure, best practices, and operational rigor to BI teams in high-growth or evolving data environments.
• Exposure to machine learning techniques such as natural language processing or time series forecasting.

We are committed to building a diverse team of Datavanters who are all responsible for stewarding a high-performance culture in which all Datavanters belong and thrive. We are proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, religion, national origin, disability, veteran status, or other legally protected status.

At Datavant our total rewards strategy powers a high-growth, high-performance, health technology company that rewards our employees for transforming health care through creating industry-defining data logistics products and services.

job_description:
About the position

Our client, an iGaming operator is seeking a Business Intelligence Analyst to join their Data team.
The Role
The Business Intelligence Analyst will support the client’s Multibrand strategies by ensuring effective replication, alignment, and optimisation of operational, lifecycle, and CRM journeys across brands.

Responsibilities
• Supporting the Multibrand strategy by replicating and troubleshooting operational and lifecycle journeys, as well as event mappings, across new brands.
• Aligning player messaging and welcome offers for newly launched brands.
• Supporting CRM cross-sell campaigns
• Assisting CRM promotional campaigns by creating ad-hoc player lists, building journeys, or collaborating with developers to introduce new journey features.
• Responding to stakeholder requests related to the creation, maintenance, and optimisation of new or existing journeys.
• Identifying and resolving root causes when journey-related issues or bugs are reported by business stakeholders.
• Interpreting journey logs and data to troubleshoot issues and optimise performance.
• Monitoring and analysing journey performance, including the development of dashboards to track key metrics and proactively identify issues.
• Collaborating closely with the development team to define and deliver new journey features and enhancements based on business requirements.

Requirements
• Some experience working with data in a commercial or fast-paced environment, ideally within iGaming or a similar digital industry.
• Strong communication skills, with the ability to engage effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
• Basic knowledge of analytical tools such as SQL or Python, and data visualisation platforms such as Tableau or similar.
• Confidence in exploring system or journey logs to identify, investigate, and understand data issues or anomalies.

Nice-to-haves
• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a relevant field (e.g. Mathematics, Statistics, Data Science, Economics, Computer Science, or a related discipline).
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with a high level of attention to detail.

job_description:
We are currently seeking an experienced and driven Lead Business Intelligence Analyst to join our dynamic team at Caterpillar. As the world's leading manufacturer of construction and mining equipment, diesel and natural gas engines, industrial gas turbines, and diesel-electric locomotives, we are constantly looking for innovative ways to improve our business operations. As the Lead Business Intelligence Analyst, you will play a crucial role in providing strategic insights and data-driven solutions to drive Caterpillar's success. We are looking for a highly analytical and detail-oriented individual who thrives in a fast-paced environment and has a passion for data and technology. If you have a strong background in business intelligence and a desire to make a significant impact in a global company, we want to hear from you!

Develop and maintain business intelligence strategies to support Caterpillar's overall goals and objectives.
Lead and mentor a team of business intelligence analysts to ensure high-quality and timely delivery of projects.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify business needs and translate them into data-driven solutions.
Conduct data analysis and provide actionable insights to inform business decision-making.
Design and implement data collection processes and systems to gather and organize relevant information.
Monitor and regularly report on key performance indicators to track business performance.
Identify and recommend opportunities for process improvement and cost savings through data analysis.
Stay updated on industry trends and advancements in business intelligence to ensure Caterpillar remains at the forefront of the industry.
Ensure data accuracy and integrity by establishing and enforcing data governance policies.
Communicate complex data and insights to non-technical stakeholders in a clear and concise manner.
Manage and prioritize multiple projects and deadlines to meet business needs.
Collaborate with IT teams to ensure seamless integration of business intelligence solutions.
Develop and maintain relationships with key stakeholders to understand business needs and requirements.
Stay updated on the latest tools and technologies in business intelligence and recommend the adoption of new tools when appropriate.
Adhere to data privacy and security policies to protect sensitive information.

Caterpillar is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.

job_description:
Datavant is the data collaboration platform trusted for healthcare. Guided by our mission to make the world's health data secure, accessible and actionable, we provide critical data solutions for organizations across the healthcare ecosystem - including providers, health plans, researchers, and life sciences companies. From fulfilling a single patient's request for their medical records to powering the AI revolution in healthcare, Datavanters are building the future of how data is connected and used to improve health.

By joining Datavant today, you're stepping onto a driven and highly collaborative team that is passionate about creating transformative change in healthcare.

At Datavant, our mission is to protect, connect, and deliver the world's health data to power better decisions and advance human health. As part of our internal team, you help us achieve this mission every day.

By continuing your career at Datavant, you'll drive transformative change in healthcare alongside a collaborative team.

The Business Intelligence & Analytics function within Datavant Product is actively seeking a detail-oriented and impact-driven data analyst to strengthen our Corporate Cross-Functional Reporting team. This pillar focuses on building a scalable, trusted reporting platform that delivers consistent, transparent, and actionable insights across our finance, accounting, compliance, and people functions. In this role you will help mature our analytics portfolio, drive BI modernization, and enable operational visibility through data. If you are an analyst who thrives at the intersection of data and business, and enjoys collaborating with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, we would love to hear from you!

You Will:

Partner & Analyze

Partner with cross-functional stakeholders to understand business needs, scope requirements, and define analytical approaches that inform decision-making.

Conduct exploratory and targeted analyses using analytical methods and business @context to generate actionable recommendations.

Design and develop business intelligence solutions, including dashboards, reports, and tools that support operational visibility and strategic insights.

Deliver Scalable BI Solutions

Lead the development and maintenance of reusable data models and visualizations, ensuring accuracy, scalability, and performance across internal and external reporting at Datavant.

Collaborate with data engineering and BI teams to ensure data quality, accessibility, and alignment between source systems and reporting layers.

Enable Continuous Improvement

Contribute to the automation of recurring analytical workflows, reducing manual reporting and improving turnaround time for business users.

Drive the growth of team practices and analytical maturity by mentoring peers, documenting solutions, and leading process improvements.

Stay current on industry best practices in data visualization, BI tools, and analytics methods to continuously elevate team capabilities.

What You Will Bring to the Table:

Technical Skills

Proficiency in statistical analysis and programming languages such as SQL and Python for data analysis and modeling.

Skill in designing clear, insightful visualizations tailored to varied audiences using tools such as Sigma, Power BI, or Tableau.

Business Acumen & Communication

Demonstrated ability to craft compelling, data-driven stories that inform strategy and influence decision-making.

Excellent communication skills with the ability to distill technical concepts for non-technical audiences.

Proven ability to manage time effectively and prioritize work to meet tight client deadlines.

Mindset & Experience

Exceptional initiative and ability to solve problems independently, seek help when needed, and take ownership when navigating ambiguity.

Experience navigating change with cross-functional collaboration, customer focus, ownership, and a bias for action.

4+ years of relevant experience in data analytics or a related field.

Bonus Points If You Have:

Experience working in healthcare, life sciences, or high-growth environments, especially in domains related to release of information (ROI) or data privacy compliance.

Understanding of data privacy and security best practices, with awareness of key regulations (e.g., HIPAA).

Experience working with cloud-based storage and computing platforms (e.g., AWS, GCP, Azure).

Experience using dbt to build and maintain data models within a modern analytics stack.

Proven ability to bring structure, best practices, and operational rigor to BI teams in high-growth or evolving data environments.

Exposure to machine learning techniques such as natural language processing or time series forecasting.

We are committed to building a diverse team of Datavanters who are all responsible for stewarding a high-performance culture in which all Datavanters belong and thrive. We are proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, religion, national origin, disability, veteran status, or other legally protected status.

job_description:
Job Description

Remote Work:

No

Job Number:

R0234650

Location:

Oklahoma City,OK,US

Share job via:

Share

Business Intelligence Analyst
The Opportunity:

At the crossroads of business and technology, innovation thrives-and as an IT Business Analyst, you'll play a key role in driving that progress. In this role, you'll collaborate with an Agile development team to design and deliver digital solutions that empower stakeholders and advance critical missions. We're seeking a proactive analyst who can translate business needs into actionable insights, explore emerging technology trends, and create intuitive, user-focused digital experiences that make a real impact.

Drive mission success through innovation. As an IT Business Analyst, you'll shape innovative digital solutions-applications, websites, systems, and products that matter. Collaborating with a multidisciplinary team of developers, testers, DevSecOps engineers, data scientists, AI/ML specialists, UX designers, and content strategists, you'll translate stakeholder needs into actionable user stories and deployable features.

On our team, you'll support Digital Airpower Solutions, working closely with key stakeholders to understand mission requirements and operational challenges. You'll leverage your knowledge of Air Force processes-including technical data, aircraft maintenance, program management, logistics, contract management, and IT compliance-to deliver solutions that meet strategic objectives.

Using tools like MS O365, Azure DevOps, SharePoint, Jira, and Balsamiq, you'll help drive tactical planning and ensure seamless execution. At Booz Allen, we believe communication and collaboration are the foundation of successful IT business analytics. Here, you'll have the resources to grow, influence meaningful missions, and help shape program strategy for the future.

Work with us to drive change in a rapidly evolving digital ecosystem as we expand capabilities for the warfighter.

Join us. The world can't wait.

You Have:
• Experience with stakeholder management
• Knowledge of Air Force aircraft acquisition and sustainment processes
• Top Secret clearance
• Bachelor's degree and 2+ years of experience in IT project management, or 5+ years experience in IT project management in lieu of a degree

Nice If You Have:
• Experience in SharePoint management
• Knowledge of Agile development processes
• Ability to demonstrate adaptability and curiosity
• Possession of excellent problem-solving skills
• Possession of excellent oral and written communication skills
• Certified Scum Master (CSM) or Project Management Professional (PMP) Certification

Clearance:

Applicants selected will be subject to a security investigation and may need to meet eligibility requirements for access to classified information; Top Secret clearance is required.

Compensation

At Booz Allen, we celebrate your contributions, provide you with opportunities and choices, and support your total well-being. Our offerings include health, life, disability, financial, and retirement benefits, as well as paid leave, professional development, tuition assistance, work-life programs, and dependent care. Our recognition awards program acknowledges employees for exceptional performance and superior demonstration of our values. Full-time and part-time employees working at least 20 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in Booz Allen's benefit programs. Individuals that do not meet the threshold are only eligible for select offerings, not inclusive of health benefits. We encourage you to learn more about our total benefits by visiting the Resource page on our Careers site and reviewing Our Employee Benefits page.

Salary at Booz Allen is determined by various factors, including but not limited to location, the individual's particular combination of education, knowledge, skills, competencies, and experience, as well as contract-specific affordability and organizational requirements. The projected compensation range for this position is $69,300.00 to $158,000.00 (annualized USD). The estimate displayed represents the typical salary range for this position and is just one component of Booz Allen's total compensation package for employees. This posting will close within 90 days from the Posting Date.

Identity Statement

As part of the application process, you are expected to be on camera during interviews and assessments. We reserve the right to take your picture to verify your identity and prevent fraud.

Work Model
Our people-first culture prioritizes the benefits of flexibility and collaboration, whether that happens in person or remotely.
• If this position is listed as remote or hybrid, you'll periodically work from a Booz Allen or client site facility.
• If this position is listed as onsite, you'll work with colleagues and clients in person, as needed for the specific role.

Commitment to Non-Discrimination

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to disability, status as a protected veteran or any other status protected by applicable federal, state, local, or international law.

Not ready to apply? Join our Talent Community and sign up for job alerts.

job_description:
A large retail & commercial bank is seeking a highly skilled, dedicated and detail-oriented BI Analyst with a strong background in business intelligence, who is passionate about making a positive impact in the financial industry. In this role, the person will be responsible for driving the data remediation efforts in the CDO. The role is preferred to be hybrid remote onsite to Quincy/Dorchester MA twice a week but for the right candidate can be fully REMOTE. As a background, the bank has close to $90B in assets, 9K employees and more than 2M customers along the east coast. In addition to providing excellent banking experiences for their customers, the bank also very involved in the community through charitable giving and philanthropy to give back to low/moderate income and underserved communities.

The Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst/Engineer will provide application analysis and data modeling design to collect data for centralized data warehouse. They will be responsible for extracting data from databases and data warehouses for reporting and facilitate sharing between multiple data systems. They will need to be proficient in the use of query and reporting analysis tools as well as standardize data collection by developing methods for database design and validation reports. Any prior professional experience transitioning from Tableau to Power BI will be great to have. Overall, the BI Analyst/Engineer will need to be proactive and demonstrate excellent communication skills as they will present in chair meetings discussing organizational data and future directions of the company.

We are a company committed to creating diverse and inclusive environments where people can bring their full, authentic selves to work every day. We are an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer that believes everyone matters. job_description:
Our job is hope.

Join a purpose-driven community of champions.

Position Purpose:

The Manager of Business Intelligence & Analytics is a hands-on leader responsible for developing, operationalizing, and maturing Rosecrance’s enterprise analytics function. This individual will serve as the critical bridge between the technical data infrastructure team and clinical, operational, and executive leaders across the organization.

The Manager will lead efforts to produce accurate, actionable, and timely insights; drive standardization of enterprise KPIs; support data quality and data lineage; and ensure alignment with Rosecrance’s emerging data governance structure. This role combines leadership, project management, analytic expertise, and strong business acumen.

Job Requirements:
• Bachelor’s degree required; preferred fields include Data Analytics, Business Intelligence, Health Informatics, Public Health, Statistics, Computer Science, or related field.
• 5 years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or healthcare analytics.
• Experience supervising staff or leading cross-functional analytic projects preferred.
• Demonstrated ability to work with large datasets and multiple IT systems.
• Proven track record using Power BI dashboards or similar visualization tools.
• Working understanding of data governance and KPI standardization.
• Familiarity with healthcare data structures.
• Ability to work both independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced
• Belief in the mission and vision of Rosecrance

Essential Responsibilities:
• Leadership & Team Management:
• Lead a team of data analysts, fostering a culture of curiosity, accuracy, accountability, and continuous improvement.
• Act as a “player-coach” to independently analyze data while also delegating, training, and reviewing the work of others.
• Build strong relationships with cross-functional leaders (clinical, operational, and financial) to understand business needs and translate them into analytic solutions.
• Business Intelligence & Analytics Execution:
• Validate and analyze large datasets across multiple systems to produce clear, actionable business insights.
• Develop and maintain dashboards, scorecards, KPI reports, and visualizations that support real-time decision-making and enterprise performance improvement.
• Proactively identify trends, risks, and opportunities from the data and communicate findings to leadership.
• Create decision-support tools that help leaders interpret data accurately and consistently.
• Data Governance & Standards:
• Serve as a support for the Data Governance Committee, helping to set standards for data quality, definitions, access, and usage.
• Partner with IT, report writers, and data engineers to document and maintain data lineage, ensuring transparency in how data flows from source systems to dashboards.
• Co-develop and maintain Rosecrance’s data dictionary, ensuring enterprise-wide clarity on data elements, definitions, and KPIs.
• Work with stakeholders to standardize enterprise KPIs and ensure consistent measurement across operating companies.
• Support the migration of reporting to Power BI and ensure alignment with enterprise data strategy.
• Collaboration With IT/Data Infrastructure:
• Collaborate closely with IT, ensuring analytic needs are aligned with data lake development, reporting architecture, and infrastructure capabilities.
• Provide business requirements and feedback to IT for data engineering, system enhancements, and reporting optimization.
• Ensure report writers and data engineers understand the operational context behind analytic requests.
• Help translate technical concepts for non-technical leaders and ensure analytics solutions are grounded in real business needs.
• Operational & Clinical Partnership:
• Work with clinical, medical, and administrative leaders to identify key metrics, outcomes, and opportunities for improvement.
• Provide analytics support for continuous quality improvement, strategic planning, and operational efficiency initiatives.
• Project Management & Strategic Execution:
• Develop project plans, timelines, and milestones for enterprise analytic initiatives.
• Prioritize analytic requests based on organizational strategy, data governance principles, and available resources.
• Ensure delivery of accurate, timely reports and dashboards that meet identified strategic priorities.
• Support leadership in using data to drive decisions, action items, and continuous improvement.
• Perform all responsibilities in compliance with the mission, vision, values, and expectations of Rosecrance.
• Deliver exceptional customer service consistently to every
• Serve as a role model for other staff, clients and customers and demonstrate positive guest relations in representing
• Assume other related responsibilities as assigned by Leadership.

Schedule: 8-hour shifts
• Shift: Monday-Friday 830a-5p
• Hybrid schedule available after training period

Pay: Based on education, experience and credentials
• Starting Pay: $120,000

Work Location: Rosecrance Rockford East– City, ST
• Onsite: The hiring manager is open to this role being located at the Rosecrance office nearest the selected candidate.

Benefits: Rosecrance values its employees and offers a comprehensive benefits package, including:
• Salary based on education, experience, and credentials
• Medical, dental, and vision insurance with multiple plan options to meet your needs
• 401(k) plan with employer match and discretionary employer contribution
• Group Life Insurance including LTD and AD&D
• Tuition assistance and licensure/certification reimbursement
• Paid Time Off, sick time, bereavement leave
• Referral program earning up to $1,000 per hire!
• Wellness plan with certain facilities offering an on-site gym
• Daily pay available through financial wellness provider: UKG Wallet

About Us:

Rosecrance has been at the forefront of providing behavioral health services for over a century. Our mission is to empower individuals and families to overcome substance abuse and mental health challenges through evidence-based therapies and compassionate care. Join us in our mission to foster lasting recovery and transform lives.

We are committed to providing careers that make a difference in the lives of the people we serve and the people we employ. We do this through the work we do, our core mission and values, our employee resources, and especially through our purpose-driven community of opportunity and hope.

Health and Safety:

Committed to upholding a drug-free environment, we prioritize the safety and well-being of both our employees and those under our care. Our zero-tolerance policy extends to all forms of drug use, including marijuana. As a part of our hiring process, candidates are required to undergo an occupational health screening, further ensuring the safety and security of our workplace community.

Equal Employment Opportunity:

Rosecrance is an equal opportunity employer and values diversity in the workplace. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, gender identity, and sexual orientation), national origin, age, disability, marital status, veteran status, or any other legally protected status. Our hiring decisions are based solely on qualifications, skills, and experience relevant to the requirements of the position.

Partnerships:

Rosecrance proudly participates in the AARP Employer Pledge Program and is a partner of MSEP (Military Spouse Employment Partnership).

Equal Opportunity Employer
This employer is required to notify all applicants of their rights pursuant to federal employment laws. For further information, please review the Know Your Rights notice from the Department of Labor.

job_description: Job posting details for Business Intelligence Analyst III at Proterra in Greer, SC, USA; Greenville County, SC, USA; South Carolina, USA; United States; North America. . ClimateTechList gathers 10,000+ job openings from over 579 climate tech companies and updates them daily.

About the position

Seeking a highly motivated Business Intelligence Analyst who will play a pivotal role in steering a dedicated team of analytics experts to support the reporting needs of the Sales & Marketing functions in our Power Efficiency Solutions (PES) segment. This role is designed for a strategic thinker who is passionate about harnessing data to drive enhanced reporting, insightful analysis, and informed decision-making. The ideal candidate will work with business leadership to determine needs, provide data insights and enhance business intelligence systems across PES. Your passion for data governance, creative problem-solving and technical skills will be crucial as you drive continuous improvement, interpret data and provide data driven recommendations and insights to accelerate business strategy and performance. This position reports directly to the Sr. Manager, Commercial Excellence for PES North America, while also supporting the needs of the segment’s VP of Sales. The Commercial Excellence team is responsible for delivering best-in-class capabilities across Commercial Analytics, Lead Generation, Sales Incentives, and CRM Management to achieve our growth objectives alongside our Sales Team. PES is one of three operating segments with approximately \$1.7 Billion in revenue and produces electric motors, variable speed controls, and air moving solutions which are sold in the residential heating and ventilating, water heating, commercial refrigeration, pump, leisure water, and other OEM and aftermarket products. Brands include Genteq® motors, as well as Fasco®, Elco®, DEC Star®, Leeson, motor and blowers.

Responsibilities
• Collaborate with business and IT teams to design, build, and deploy Power BI solutions to commercial needs.
• Mentor team as a subject matter expert in PowerBI.
• Analyze large amounts of information to discover and report on trends and patterns.
• Create visualizations and reports for requested projects.
• Conduct testing and troubleshooting of Power BI solutions.
• Support the team as a subject matter expert in Power BI.

Requirements
• Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Data Science, Computer Science, Business or related field.
• 5-7 years' experience in business analyst role with a focus on BI
• Ability to synthesize and effectively communicate performance based upon information from disparate sources.
• Strong presentation communication skills with the ability to summarize and communicate data clearly and accurately.
• Fluent in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).
• Ability to engage and build value-based relationships at all levels and functions
• High level of personal initiative, energy and ownership of business results.
• Ability to manage multiple projects and work to tight deadlines.

Nice-to-haves
• Power BI certifications.
• Familiarity with Salesforce.com reporting and integration.
• Experience with Salesforce.com reporting, SQL, Microsoft Access, Excel, and Power Apps, and Oracle integration, preferred
• Commercial experience aligned Sales and Customer Care data and processes strongly preferred
• Knowledge of data structure best practices - data warehousing, modeling, and mining.
• Project management experience is a plus.
• Ability to synthesize and effectively communicate organizational performance based upon information from disparate sources

Benefits
• Medical, Dental, Vision and Prescription Drug Coverage
• Spending accounts (HSA, Health Care FSA and Dependent Care FSA)
• Paid Time Off and Holidays
• 401k Retirement Plan with Matching Employer Contributions
• Life and Accidental Death & Dismemberment (AD&D) Insurance
• Paid Leaves
• Tuition Assistance

job_description: We're looking for a strategic and technically sharp BI Analyst to help shape how we measure, optimize, and scale our global SDR organization. In this role, you'll own our SDR analytics framework from building dashboards to deploying predictive models—and partner closely with Sales Development, RevOps, and Finance leadership. This is a high-impact opportunity for someone who thrives at the intersection of data, GTM strategy, and automation. If you're fluent in SQL, comfortable in Snowflake, and curious about how AI and ML can supercharge sales performance, we want to meet you. What You'll Do Build and maintain dynamic dashboards, forecasting models, and performance tools to drive SDR productivity. Identify trends, bottlenecks, and opportunities across pipeline generation, conversion funnels, and rep performance. Develop motion-level P&L views connecting SDR activity to ROI, CAC, and pipeline contribution. Partner with RevOps, Campaigns, and Finance to measure campaign effectiveness and optimize GTM investments. Leverage ML models or AI tools to surface predictive insights and automate repetitive analytics tasks. Define and document key metrics, data workflows, and KPI ownership in a self-serve environment (Notion/Sigma). Present actionable insights and strategic recommendations to stakeholders and executive leadership. Your Background 3–5 years of experience in BI, RevOps, or data analytics supporting sales or GTM teams. Advanced SQL skills and hands-on experience with Snowflake, Looker/Looker Studio , and Excel/Google Sheets. Experience with Sigma, dbt, and modern data stack tools like Airflow, Fivetran, or Hightouch is a plus. Strong understanding of sales funnels, forecasting, and pipeline attribution in a B2B SaaS environment. Familiarity with AI/ML frameworks or agents used for automation, predictions, or reporting is a big plus. Excellent communication and collaboration skills across technical and non-technical stakeholders. Comfortable operating in remote, fast-paced environments. Your First 90 Days Audit existing SDR dashboards and rebuild core reporting on performance, pacing, and pipeline conversion. Align with Finance on SDR cost models, ROI attribution, and motion-level investment impact. job at Workday, Inc.. Atlanta, GA.
