job_description:
About the position

At American Vision Partners (AVP) , we partner with the most respected ophthalmology practices in the country and integrate best-in-class management systems, operational infrastructure, and advanced technology to provide the highest quality patient care possible. Our practices include Barnet Dulaney Perkins Eye Center, Southwestern Eye Center, Retinal Consultants of Arizona, M&M Eye Institute, Abrams Eye Institute, Southwest Eye Institute, Aiello Eye Institute, Moretsky Cassidy Vision Correction, Wellish Vision Institute, West Texas Eye Associates and Vantage Eye Center. We are focused on building the nation’s largest and most comprehensive eye care practices and currently operate more than 100 eye care centers in Arizona, New Mexico, Nevada, California and Texas – including 25 ambulatory surgical centers. At AVP we value teamwork, providing exceptional experiences, continuous improvement, financial strength, and hard work. We are committed to providing best-in-class patient care, pioneering research and technology, and most importantly, rewarding and recognizing our employees! Overview As a BI Analyst- Financial Planning and Analysis, you will use data to catch the pulse of current market trends to better assist our company with profits. You will work with data from multiple sources including the company database, web database, data from other companies, etc. You will then collect the data and use it to create a picture of the current market and the best path for a company to take in the future.

Responsibilities
• Use SQL to develop queries to analyze big datasets, validate their quality and visualize outcomes
• Work across various business departments to define and document business and technical requirements for data analysis and dashboards
• Develop and maintain databases, data sets, and data processes to help drive business intelligence
• Utilize financial and operational modeling to drive strategy business decisions
• Present analytical findings and recommendations to operational leaders and senior leadership
• Actively work to understand and refine existing processes to develop new analytics views
• All Other Duties as assigned

Requirements
• BA/BS or HS and equivalent work history
• 4+ year in a similar role
• 2+ years developing and writing SQL queries
• Multi site experience required
• Strong understanding of relational database foundations- creating, structuring, maintaining, and updating databases.
• Proven experience in strategic analytics and modeling
• Proven experience in leveraging big data
• Ability to uncover operational and data trends
• Knowledge of Excel, PowerPoint, data warehousing (Sequel), data mining, business intelligence (Power BI), and other financial systems
• Excellent written and oral communication
• Self-motivated with the ability to work independently in a fast-paced, high pressure, and rapidly changing environment

Nice-to-haves
• Healthcare Experience highly preferred

Benefits
• Your health, happiness and your future matters!
• At AVP, we offer everything from medical and dental insurance, significant eye care discounts, child care assistance, pet insurance, continuing education funds, 401(k), paid holidays, PTO, Sick Time, opportunity for growth, and much more!

job_description:
Datavant is the data collaboration platform trusted for healthcare. Guided by our mission to make the world’s health data secure, accessible and actionable, we provide critical data solutions for organizations across the healthcare ecosystem - including providers, health plans, researchers, and life sciences companies. From fulfilling a single patient’s request for their medical records to powering the AI revolution in healthcare, Datavanters are building the future of how data is connected and used to improve health.

By joining Datavant today, you’re stepping onto a driven and highly collaborative team that is passionate about creating transformative change in healthcare.

At Datavant, our mission is to protect, connect, and deliver the world’s health data to power better decisions and advance human health. As part of our internal team, you help us achieve this mission every day.

By continuing your career at Datavant, you’ll drive transformative change in healthcare alongside a collaborative team.

The Business Intelligence & Analytics function within Datavant Product is actively seeking a detail-oriented and impact-driven data analyst to strengthen our Corporate Cross-Functional Reporting team. This pillar focuses on building a scalable, trusted reporting platform that delivers consistent, transparent, and actionable insights across our finance, accounting, compliance, and people functions. In this role you will help mature our analytics portfolio, drive BI modernization, and enable operational visibility through data. If you are an analyst who thrives at the intersection of data and business, and enjoys collaborating with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, we would love to hear from you!

You Will:
• Partner & Analyze
• Partner with cross-functional stakeholders to understand business needs, scope requirements, and define analytical approaches that inform decision-making.
• Conduct exploratory and targeted analyses using analytical methods and business context to generate actionable recommendations.
• Design and develop business intelligence solutions, including dashboards, reports, and tools that support operational visibility and strategic insights.
• Deliver Scalable BI Solutions
• Lead the development and maintenance of reusable data models and visualizations, ensuring accuracy, scalability, and performance across internal and external reporting at Datavant.
• Collaborate with data engineering and BI teams to ensure data quality, accessibility, and alignment between source systems and reporting layers.
• Enable Continuous Improvement
• Contribute to the automation of recurring analytical workflows, reducing manual reporting and improving turnaround time for business users.
• Drive the growth of team practices and analytical maturity by mentoring peers, documenting solutions, and leading process improvements.
• Stay current on industry best practices in data visualization, BI tools, and analytics methods to continuously elevate team capabilities.

What You Will Bring to the Table:
• Technical Skills
• Proficiency in statistical analysis and programming languages such as SQL and Python for data analysis and modeling.
• Skill in designing clear, insightful visualizations tailored to varied audiences using tools such as Sigma, Power BI, or Tableau.
• Business Acumen & Communication
• Demonstrated ability to craft compelling, data-driven stories that inform strategy and influence decision-making.
• Excellent communication skills with the ability to distill technical concepts for non-technical audiences.
• Proven ability to manage time effectively and prioritize work to meet tight client deadlines.
• Mindset & Experience
• Exceptional initiative and ability to solve problems independently, seek help when needed, and take ownership when navigating ambiguity.
• Experience navigating change with cross-functional collaboration, customer focus, ownership, and a bias for action.
• 4+ years of relevant experience in data analytics or a related field.

Bonus Points If You Have:
• Experience working in healthcare, life sciences, or high-growth environments, especially in domains related to release of information (ROI) or data privacy compliance.
• Understanding of data privacy and security best practices, with awareness of key regulations (e.g., HIPAA).
• Experience working with cloud-based storage and computing platforms (e.g., AWS, GCP, Azure).
• Experience using dbt to build and maintain data models within a modern analytics stack.
• Proven ability to bring structure, best practices, and operational rigor to BI teams in high-growth or evolving data environments.
• Exposure to machine learning techniques such as natural language processing or time series forecasting.

We are committed to building a diverse team of Datavanters who are all responsible for stewarding a high-performance culture in which all Datavanters belong and thrive. We are proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, religion, national origin, disability, veteran status, or other legally protected status.

At Datavant our total rewards strategy powers a high-growth, high-performance, health technology company that rewards our employees for transforming health care through creating industry-defining data logistics products and services.

$104,000—$125,000 USD

Depending on the state in which you will be working, exemptions may be available on the basis of disability, medical contraindications to the vaccine or any of its components, pregnancy or pregnancy-related medical conditions, and/or religion.

job_title: US - AML Analytics Specialist (Power BI / Tableau)
job_description:
Job Title: AML Analytics Specialist (Power BI / Tableau)
Location: Remote (U.S.-based)
Job Type: Full-Time / Contract

About the Role:
We are looking for a skilled and data-driven AML Analytics Specialist with expertise in Power BI or Tableau to join our U.S.-based remote team. This role is ideal for someone with a strong background in Anti-Money Laundering (AML) analytics and a passion for turning complex compliance data into compelling, actionable visual insights.

As a key member of our compliance analytics function, you will be responsible for building interactive dashboards and reports that support regulatory requirements, risk assessments, and strategic decision-making across financial crime prevention initiatives.

Key Responsibilities:
• Design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and analytics solutions using Power BI or Tableau
• Visualize complex AML and financial crime data in a clear, intuitive, and impactful manner
• Interpret detection scenarios, risk scoring models, and regulatory data for reporting and insights
• Collaborate with compliance, risk, and data teams to define reporting needs and deliver actionable insights
• Ensure data accuracy, visual clarity, and compliance with U.S. AML regulations
• Support strategic compliance initiatives through data storytelling and trend analysis

Requirements:
• Proven experience in AML or financial crime analytics with a strong data visualization focus
• Advanced proficiency in Power BI or Tableau, including dashboards, data modeling, and performance tuning
• Solid understanding of AML regulations (e.g., BSA, OFAC), typologies, and risk assessment models
• Ability to distill complex information into clear, concise visual reports for a range of stakeholders
• Strong communication skills and a proactive, solution-oriented mindset

Nice to Have:
• SQL knowledge or experience with data transformation tools
• Background in regulated financial services environments
• Understanding of data governance and compliance reporting requirements

What We Offer:
• Fully remote role within a collaborative, U.S.-based team
• Competitive pay and flexible working hours
• A meaningful opportunity to help fight financial crime through impactful data storytelling

Visualize complex AML and financial crime data in a clear, intuitive, and impactful manner
Interpret detection scenarios, risk scoring models, and regulatory data for reporting and insights
Collaborate with compliance, risk, and data teams to define reporting needs and deliver actionable insights
Ensure data accuracy, visual clarity, and compliance with U.S. AML regulations
Support strategic compliance initiatives through data storytelling and trend analysis

job_description:
Job Summary

We are seeking an experienced Senior Data Analyst with 8+ years of experience in the Insurance domain, specifically working with non-claims data (policy, underwriting, billing, customer, finance, etc.). The ideal candidate should have strong expertise in SQL and Tableau (or similar BI tools) to perform advanced data analysis, reporting, and business insights generation.

Key Responsibilities
• Analyze large datasets related to policy administration, underwriting, billing, renewals, and customer data.
• Develop and optimize complex SQL queries for data extraction, transformation, and validation.
• Design and build interactive dashboards and reports using Tableau or similar BI tools (Power BI, Qlik, etc.).
• Work closely with business stakeholders (Underwriting, Finance, Operations) to gather reporting requirements.
• Perform data validation, reconciliation, and ensure data quality.
• Identify trends, KPIs, and actionable insights to support strategic decision-making.
• Support regulatory and management reporting requirements.
• Collaborate with data engineering teams for data pipeline and model improvements.
• Document data definitions, business logic, and reporting standards.

Required Skills & Qualifications
• 8+ years of experience as a Data Analyst in the Insurance domain (Non-Claims focus).
• Strong hands-on experience with SQL (advanced queries, joins, subqueries, CTEs, performance tuning).
• Experience with Tableau or other visualization tools (Power BI, QlikView, Looker).
• Strong understanding of insurance policy lifecycle and non-claims processes.
• Experience working with large relational databases (SQL Server, Oracle, etc.).
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
• Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.

job_description:
Join to apply for the Contract Supply Chain BI Analyst role at Revelyst

Join to apply for the Contract Supply Chain BI Analyst role at Revelyst

Get AI-powered advice on this job and more exclusive features.

Revelyst, is a collective of makers who design and manufacture performance gear and precision technologies. Our category-defining brands leverage meticulous craftsmanship and cross-collaboration to pursue innovation and redefine what is humanly possible in the outdoors.

This position reports to the Sr Manager Analytics, GOSC and is based out of our Hyde Park, UT office.

As the Contract Supply Chain BI Analyst you will have an opportunity to work on:
• OTIF Dashboard Optimization: Review current dashboard, data structures, and calculations to optimize dashboard performance and finalize phase 2 product. Document final dashboard calculations and translations.
• EDW Requirements: Assist with evaluation, definition, and implementation of Global Operations and Supply Chain KPI data needs within corporate EDW environment. Review existing infrastructure and identify opportunities to automate Level 1-3 KPIs.
• OTIF Dashboard Migration: Upon delivery of enterprise EDW the analyst will update the semantic layer of the OTIF Dashboard, reconfigure calculations to new data structure, test results, and publish a finished product.
• Order Management Dashboard Deployment: Develop & Deploy dashboard displaying Sales order/invoice data across Revelyst. Working with Sales Ops to understand current reporting processes, data sources, order status definitions, and consolidate to one unified dashboard.
• Change Management: Develop and execute change management strategies that ensure stakeholder buy-in and successful adoption of new systems and processes

You Have
• 5+ years of experience in data analytics or business intelligence, preferably developing dashboards supporting global supply chain or operations.
• Advanced technical skills in SQL, Power BI, Azure-Databricks.
• Strong understanding of end-to-end supply chain processes (PLAN, MAKE, SOURCE, DELIVER) and ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, NetSuite).

Pay Range

Starting Hourly Rate: $60.00

The actual annual salary offered to a candidate will be based on variables including experience, geographic location, education, and skills/achievements, and will be mutually agreed upon at the time of offer.

We offer a highly competitive salary, comprehensive benefits including: medical and dental, vision, disability and life insurance, 401K, PTO, paid holidays, gear discounts and the ability to add value to an exciting mission!

Our Postings are not intended for distribution to or use in any jurisdiction, country or territory where such distribution or use would violate local law or would subject us to any regulations in another jurisdiction, country or territory. We reserve the right to limit our Postings in any jurisdiction, country or territory.

Job Summary
:
The Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst – Healthcare Applications plays a critical role in supporting healthcare operations by building forms, reports, and widgets within vendor-hosted Electronic Health Record (EHR) systems. This position emphasizes collaboration with change requesters from the Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS), ensuring clear communication and understanding of requirements. The BI Analyst is responsible for tracking and commenting on work progress, maintaining transparency throughout the development cycle.

Strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple priorities are essential for success in this role.

• Complete complex healthcare-related report requests, including interviewing requestors, understanding goals, performing data queries, analyzing and summarizing data, and presenting results to technical and non-technical audiences.
• Utilizes programming tools to assist in data extraction from multiple data sources and transformation to support creating usable and reportable formats for final presentation and to make reporting more efficient and timelier.

• Enhance reporting tools using SQL and ensure data integrity through quality assurance processes.
• Mentor and train end users and Analysts in healthcare data processes, structures, and validation steps.
• Work within a vendor-hosted Electronic Health Record (EHR) system to build forms, reports, and widgets
• Update Jira ticketing system and maintain clear communication with requesters regarding status and requirements.
• Apply project management skills to healthcare-related projects, ensuring timely completion of tasks and communication.
• Participates in other departmental project activities and tasks to ensure successful completion of project, data collection, data management, communication, and data reporting.
• Complete all tasks required for the position, as assigned by the Supervisor or Manager.
• Current resident of the State of WI.

The Claims Business Intelligence Senior Analyst is responsible for delivering comprehensive analysis and actionable insights to support the Claims organization. This role leverages deep expertise in data analysis, reporting, and visualization to drive strategic process improvements, inform executive decision-making, and enhance claims outcomes.

Primary Responsibilities:
• Support data needs of Claims Actuarial team for their monthly and quarterly reports. Implement new data splits and improvements as needed.
• Design, develop, and automate advanced dashboards and reports leveraging BI tools, strategic data products and latest technology.
• Analyze large, complex claims datasets to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for operational and strategic improvement.
• Lead high-impact, cross-functional projects, support claims data committee, and fulfill ad hoc data analytics requests.
• Collaborate with Claims, IT, and cross-functional business units to gather requirements and translate business needs into robust analytical solutions. Verify that key data attributes are accurate and meet reporting needs.
• Present complex findings and strategic recommendations to stakeholders at various levels.
• Stay abreast of industry trends, best practices, and emerging BI technologies.
• Manage and mentor 1-2 onshore or offshore junior analysts or BI developers.
Chubb is a world leader in insurance. With operations in 54 countries, Chubb provides commercial and personal property and casualty insurance, personal accident and supplemental health insurance, reinsurance, and life insurance to a diverse group of clients. The company is distinguished by its extensive product and service offerings, broad distribution capabilities, exceptional financial strength, underwriting excellence, superior claims handling expertise and local operations globally.

At Chubb, we are committed to equal employment opportunity and compliance with all laws and regulations pertaining to it. Our policy is to provide employment, training, compensation, promotion, and other conditions or opportunities of employment, without regard to race, color, religious creed, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, national origin, ancestry, mental and physical disability, medical condition, genetic information, military and veteran status, age, and pregnancy or any other characteristic protected by law. Performance and qualifications are the only basis upon which we hire, assign, promote, compensate, develop and retain employees. Chubb prohibits all unlawful discrimination, harassment and retaliation against any individual who reports discrimination or harassment.
• Minimum of a bachelor's degree in Statistics, Applied Mathematics, Business Analytics, Management Information Systems, Computer Science, or related fields
• 5+ years of experience in P&C insurance industry focusing on data-driven decision-making; Claims data & analytics experience a plus
• Proficiency in BI tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau, Qlik), programming language (SQL, R, Python), and Excel (advanced functions)
• Strong analytical, problem-solving, critical thinking, and time management, and organizational skills
• Results-oriented with proven track records of successful deliverables with measurable impact, keen attention to details
• Excellent communication skills, with the ability to convey complex data concepts and analytical findings to both technical and non-technical audiences
• Proven ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and deliver results in a fast-paced environment

Welcome to HCA Healthcare! We are seeking a highly skilled Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst to join our dynamic team. As a BI Analyst, you will play a crucial role in analyzing large sets of data to provide valuable insights and recommendations to drive strategic business decisions. This is a perfect opportunity for a detail-oriented and analytical individual who thrives in a fast-paced and collaborative environment. If you have a passion for data and a strong understanding of business operations, we encourage you to apply for this exciting opportunity at HCA Healthcare.

Conduct data analysis: Utilize advanced data analysis techniques to gather insights and identify trends from large sets of data.

Continuously improve processes: Identify areas for improvement in data analysis processes and make recommendations for optimization.

Strive for continuous learning: Continuously learn and develop skills in data analysis and business intelligence to stay current with industry advancements.

job_description:
Datavant is the data collaboration platform trusted for healthcare. Guided by our mission to make the world's health data secure, accessible and actionable, we provide critical data solutions for organizations across the healthcare ecosystem - including providers, health plans, researchers, and life sciences companies. From fulfilling a single patient's request for their medical records to powering the AI revolution in healthcare, Datavanters are building the future of how data is connected and used to improve health.

By joining Datavant today, you're stepping onto a driven and highly collaborative team that is passionate about creating transformative change in healthcare.

At Datavant, our mission is to protect, connect, and deliver the world's health data to power better decisions and advance human health. As part of our internal team, you help us achieve this mission every day.

By continuing your career at Datavant, you'll drive transformative change in healthcare alongside a collaborative team.

The Business Intelligence & Analytics function within Datavant Product is actively seeking a detail-oriented and impact-driven data analyst to strengthen our Corporate Cross-Functional Reporting team. This pillar focuses on building a scalable, trusted reporting platform that delivers consistent, transparent, and actionable insights across our finance, accounting, compliance, and people functions. In this role you will help mature our analytics portfolio, drive BI modernization, and enable operational visibility through data. If you are an analyst who thrives at the intersection of data and business, and enjoys collaborating with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, we would love to hear from you!
• *You Will:**

+ Partner & Analyze

+ Partner with cross-functional stakeholders to understand business needs, scope requirements, and define analytical approaches that inform decision-making.

+ Conduct exploratory and targeted analyses using analytical methods and business context to generate actionable recommendations.

+ Design and develop business intelligence solutions, including dashboards, reports, and tools that support operational visibility and strategic insights.

+ Deliver Scalable BI Solutions

+ Lead the development and maintenance of reusable data models and visualizations, ensuring accuracy, scalability, and performance across internal and external reporting at Datavant.

job_description:
Datavant is the data collaboration platform trusted for healthcare. Guided by our mission to make the world's health data secure, accessible and actionable, we provide critical data solutions for organizations across the healthcare ecosystem - including providers, health plans, researchers, and life sciences companies. From fulfilling a single patient's request for their medical records to powering the AI revolution in healthcare, Datavanters are building the future of how data is connected and used to improve health.

By joining Datavant today, you're stepping onto a driven and highly collaborative team that is passionate about creating transformative change in healthcare.

At Datavant, our mission is to protect, connect, and deliver the world's health data to power better decisions and advance human health. As part of our internal team, you help us achieve this mission every day.

By continuing your career at Datavant, you'll drive transformative change in healthcare alongside a collaborative team.

The Business Intelligence & Analytics function within Datavant Product is actively seeking a detail-oriented and impact-driven data analyst to strengthen our Corporate Cross-Functional Reporting team. This pillar focuses on building a scalable, trusted reporting platform that delivers consistent, transparent, and actionable insights across our finance, accounting, compliance, and people functions. In this role you will help mature our analytics portfolio, drive BI modernization, and enable operational visibility through data. If you are an analyst who thrives at the intersection of data and business, and enjoys collaborating with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, we would love to hear from you!
• *You Will:**

+ Partner & Analyze

+ Partner with cross-functional stakeholders to understand business needs, scope requirements, and define analytical approaches that inform decision-making.

+ Conduct exploratory and targeted analyses using analytical methods and business context to generate actionable recommendations.

+ Design and develop business intelligence solutions, including dashboards, reports, and tools that support operational visibility and strategic insights.

+ Deliver Scalable BI Solutions

+ Lead the development and maintenance of reusable data models and visualizations, ensuring accuracy, scalability, and performance across internal and external reporting at Datavant.

+ Collaborate with data engineering and BI teams to ensure data quality, accessibility, and alignment between source systems and reporting layers.

+ Enable Continuous Improvement

+ Contribute to the automation of recurring analytical workflows, reducing manual reporting and improving turnaround time for business users.

+ Drive the growth of team practices and analytical maturity by mentoring peers, documenting solutions, and leading process improvements.

+ Stay current on industry best practices in data visualization, BI tools, and analytics methods to continuously elevate team capabilities.
• *What You Will Bring to the Table:**

+ Technical Skills

+ Proficiency in statistical analysis and programming languages such as SQL and Python for data analysis and modeling.

+ Skill in designing clear, insightful visualizations tailored to varied audiences using tools such as Sigma, Power BI, or Tableau.

+ Business Acumen & Communication

+ Demonstrated ability to craft compelling, data-driven stories that inform strategy and influence decision-making.

+ Excellent communication skills with the ability to distill technical concepts for non-technical audiences.

+ Proven ability to manage time effectively and prioritize work to meet tight client deadlines.

+ Mindset & Experience

+ Exceptional initiative and ability to solve problems independently, seek help when needed, and take ownership when navigating ambiguity.

+ Experience navigating change with cross-functional collaboration, customer focus, ownership, and a bias for action.

+ 4+ years of relevant experience in data analytics or a related field.
• *Bonus Points If You Have:**

+ Experience working in healthcare, life sciences, or high-growth environments, especially in domains related to release of information (ROI) or data privacy compliance.

+ Understanding of data privacy and security best practices, with awareness of key regulations (e.g., HIPAA).

+ Experience working with cloud-based storage and computing platforms (e.g., AWS, GCP, Azure).

+ Experience using dbt to build and maintain data models within a modern analytics stack.

+ Proven ability to bring structure, best practices, and operational rigor to BI teams in high-growth or evolving data environments.

+ Exposure to machine learning techniques such as natural language processing or time series forecasting.

job_description:
Student Success BI Analyst III

Salary:
$62,542.00 - $73,579.00 Annually

Job Type:
FT Exempt Salaried Staff

Job Number:
FY2504487

Closing:
8/6/2025 11:59 PM Mountain

Location:
800 W University Parkway, Orem

Division:
VP Student Affairs

Position Announcement

Utah Valley University is seeking a collaborative and data-savvy professional to join our team as a Student Success BI Analyst. This role offers the opportunity to make a meaningful impact by turning data into actionable insights that directly support student engagement, outreach, and retention. Working closely with Student Affairs and Business Intelligence and Research Services (BIRS), the analyst will play a key role in leading student success data initiatives, developing interactive dashboards, and guiding data-informed strategies across campus.

This is a great opportunity for someone who is passionate about using data to drive positive change in higher education. The ideal candidate will enjoy cross-functional collaboration, have a strong attention to detail, and be committed to ensuring data integrity and compliance. If you're excited about shaping data-driven solutions that support students' academic journeys, we encourage you to apply.

Summary of Responsibilities

• Project Leadership / Stakeholder Management Provides project leadership and coordination for data-informed student success initiatives. Collaborates with advisors, faculty, and student service t
eams to understand data needs and translate insights from the Student Success Management System (SSMS) into actionable outreach campaigns that support retention and engagement. Assists campus stakeholders in formulating data requests, interpreting student success metrics, and using available tools to support decision-making and strategic outreach.

• Analysis Collects, analyzes, and interprets data from the SSMS and other internal sources to identify trends, patterns, and risk indicators that inform student support strategies. Produces actionable dashboards, visualizations, and reports that communicate key student success indicators, track campaign effectiveness, and support continuous improvement efforts. Identifies areas of opportunity or concern through root cause analysis and presents findings to stakeholders to guide strategic interventions.

• Reporting and Tools Designs and develops reports and dashboards that enable campus partners to monitor student engagement, persistence, and success. Creates tools and templates to streamline reporting from the SSMS and ensure consistency and usability across departments. Ensures alignment with institutional data governance practices and contributes to enhancements in student success data tools.

• Professional Development and Best Practices Maintains knowledge of emerging trends and best practices in student success analytics, data visualization, predictive modeling, and data-informed outreach strategies. Participates in communities of practice and professional development to improve the use of data in student support.

• Other Duties as Assigned Performs additional duties related to student success data and outreach as assigned, including participation in cross-functional committees, task forces, or data projects.

Qualifications / Licenses / Certifications

Graduation from an accredited institution with a bachelor's degree and one year of related experience in data analysis, data reporting, or student success technology in higher education, or any combination of education and relevant experience totaling five years.
Preferred Qualifications
Master's degree in a relevant field.

Knowledge / Skills / Abilities

Knowledge

• St
udent Success Management Systems (e.g., Navigate, Starfish, Salesforce Education Cloud) and their role in student retention and support.

• Higher education st dent support services, advising models, and retention strategies.

• Data governance practices, FERPA, and institutional policies related to data security and privacy.

• Principles and practices of data analysis, including trend analysis, root cause analysis, and predictive modeling.

• Business intelligence tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar reporting and dashboard platforms.

• SQL and/or other query languages used for extracting and manipulating data.

Skills

• Designing and delivering clear, actionable data visualizations and reports for diverse stakeholders.

• Collaborating with cross-functional teams including advisors, faculty, institutional research, and IT professionals.

• Leading data-informed projects and coordinating outreach or engagement campaigns.

• Translating complex data into meaningful insights and strategic recommendations.

• Managing multiple priorities and meeting deadlines in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.

• Effectively communicating data findings both orally and in writing to technical and non-technical audiences.

Abilities

• Interpret student success metrics to identify risk factors, trends, and opportunities for strategic intervention.

• Work independently with minimal supervision while also functioning effectively in a collaborative team setting.

• Maintain high attention to detail while managing complex data sets.

• Uphold confidentiality and ethical standards in handling sensitive student data.

• Stay current on best practices in student success analytics, data visualization, and data-informed decision-making.

• Adapt tools and approaches to meet the evolving needs of student affairs and institutional strategy.

EEO Statement:

UVU employment decisions are made on the basis of an applicant's qualifications and ability to perform the job without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age (40 and over), disability, veteran status, pregnancy, childbirth, or pregnancy-related conditions, genetic information, or other bases protected by applicable federal, state, or local law.

To apply, please visit https://apptrkr.com/6416041

About the Company:
UTAH VALLEY UNIVERSITY

Utah Valley University was established in 1941 as Central Utah Vocational School (CUVS) with the primary function of providing war production training. CUVS was part of the Provo School District located in south Provo. The institution received a state appropriation in March 1945 of $50,000 to operate for the 1945-1947 biennium. In 1947, the school received funding as a permanent state institution.

A new site for the school was acquired on University Avenue in Provo in 1948; in the 1952, the state appropriated funding for the first construction on that site. As enrollments grew, the state acquired over 185 acres in southwest Orem and the first building was completed in 1977. Today, the University’s facilities consist of a combined total of 412 acres with 50 buildings with campuses in Orem, Provo, and Heber City and property in Vineyard and at Thanksgiving Point in Lehi.

job_description:
Job Summary

Homeland has partnered with an innovative company that is transforming the healthcare insurance landscape through a connected, data-driven ecosystem designed to simplify complexity and drive long-term success. We are seeking a Data & Reporting Analyst to join our team.

The Data & Reporting Analyst is a key member of the data team and reports directly to the Director of Data Analytics. This role is responsible for analyzing data, identifying trends, forecasting outcomes, and translating insights into meaningful, actionable recommendations that support business decision-making.

The Data & Reporting Analyst will design, build, and maintain reports, dashboards, KPIs, and visualizations that highlight performance trends, patterns, and anomalies across the organization. In addition, this role will develop and implement data collection systems and strategies to improve efficiency, accuracy, and overall data quality.

The ideal candidate is technical, creative, and self-motivated, with a strong analytical mindset and the ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment while collaborating effectively with cross-functional teams.

Responsibilities
• Develop, maintain, and enhance dashboards and reports across multiple departments, including Sales, Finance, and Operations
• Analyze data to identify trends, gaps, and inconsistencies; reconcile datasets to produce accurate, actionable insights
• Present findings using key company metrics and develop tracking and reporting tools for stakeholders
• Create ad-hoc reports and presentations to support management and executive decision-making
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather, document, and translate reporting and analytics requirements
• Ensure data accuracy, integrity, and overall quality across all reporting and analytics processes

Requirements
• Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Business, Engineering, or a related field
• 2–5 years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or a related role
• Proven experience with data analysis, reporting, and dashboard development
• Advanced proficiency in SQL and Python, along with data visualization tools such as Tableau and Power BI
• Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel for cleaning, transforming, and analyzing large datasets
• Working knowledge of Microsoft Azure and Microsoft Fabric environments
• Strong analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills with exceptional attention to detail
• Self-motivated and capable of working independently while managing multiple projects simultaneously
• Excellent time management skills with the ability to prioritize competing workstreams in a fast-paced environment
• Strong communication and collaboration skills, with experience working across cross-functional teams

Benefits

This is a hybrid role offering a competitive salary range of $110,000–$125,000, plus a 10% bonus opportunity and a comprehensive benefits package.

Summary

Apply today!

EEO Notice

Homeland LLC is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Homeland LLC provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, marital status, amnesty, or status as a covered veteran in accordance with applicable federal, state, and local laws. Homeland LLC complies with applicable state and local laws governing nondiscrimination in employment in every location in which the company has facilities. Reasonable accommodation is available for qualified individuals with disabilities, upon request.

job_description:
Datavant is the data collaboration platform trusted for healthcare. Guided by our mission to make the world’s health data secure, accessible and actionable, we provide critical data solutions for organizations across the healthcare ecosystem - including providers, health plans, researchers, and life sciences companies. From fulfilling a single patient’s request for their medical records to powering the AI revolution in healthcare, Datavanters are building the future of how data is connected and used to improve health.

By joining Datavant today, you’re stepping onto a driven and highly collaborative team that is passionate about creating transformative change in healthcare.

At Datavant, our mission is to protect, connect, and deliver the world’s health data to power better decisions and advance human health. As part of our internal team, you help us achieve this mission every day.

By continuing your career at Datavant, you’ll drive transformative change in healthcare alongside a collaborative team.

The Business Intelligence & Analytics function within Datavant Product is actively seeking a detail-oriented and impact-driven data analyst to strengthen our Corporate Cross-Functional Reporting team. This pillar focuses on building a scalable, trusted reporting platform that delivers consistent, transparent, and actionable insights across our finance, accounting, compliance, and people functions. In this role you will help mature our analytics portfolio, drive BI modernization, and enable operational visibility through data. If you are an analyst who thrives at the intersection of data and business, and enjoys collaborating with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, we would love to hear from you!

You Will:
• Partner & Analyze
• Partner with cross-functional stakeholders to understand business needs, scope requirements, and define analytical approaches that inform decision-making.
• Conduct exploratory and targeted analyses using analytical methods and business context to generate actionable recommendations.
• Design and develop business intelligence solutions, including dashboards, reports, and tools that support operational visibility and strategic insights.
• Deliver Scalable BI Solutions
• Lead the development and maintenance of reusable data models and visualizations, ensuring accuracy, scalability, and performance across internal and external reporting at Datavant.
• Collaborate with data engineering and BI teams to ensure data quality, accessibility, and alignment between source systems and reporting layers.
• Enable Continuous Improvement
• Contribute to the automation of recurring analytical workflows, reducing manual reporting and improving turnaround time for business users.
• Drive the growth of team practices and analytical maturity by mentoring peers, documenting solutions, and leading process improvements.
• Stay current on industry best practices in data visualization, BI tools, and analytics methods to continuously elevate team capabilities.

What You Will Bring to the Table:
• Technical Skills
• Proficiency in statistical analysis and programming languages such as SQL and Python for data analysis and modeling.
• Skill in designing clear, insightful visualizations tailored to varied audiences using tools such as Sigma, Power BI, or Tableau.
• Business Acumen & Communication
• Demonstrated ability to craft compelling, data-driven stories that inform strategy and influence decision-making.
• Excellent communication skills with the ability to distill technical concepts for non-technical audiences.
• Proven ability to manage time effectively and prioritize work to meet tight client deadlines.
• Mindset & Experience
• Exceptional initiative and ability to solve problems independently, seek help when needed, and take ownership when navigating ambiguity.
• Experience navigating change with cross-functional collaboration, customer focus, ownership, and a bias for action.
• 4+ years of relevant experience in data analytics or a related field.

Bonus Points If You Have:
• Experience working in healthcare, life sciences, or high-growth environments, especially in domains related to release of information (ROI) or data privacy compliance.
• Understanding of data privacy and security best practices, with awareness of key regulations (e.g., HIPAA).
• Experience working with cloud-based storage and computing platforms (e.g., AWS, GCP, Azure).
• Experience using dbt to build and maintain data models within a modern analytics stack.
• Proven ability to bring structure, best practices, and operational rigor to BI teams in high-growth or evolving data environments.
• Exposure to machine learning techniques such as natural language processing or time series forecasting.

We are committed to building a diverse team of Datavanters who are all responsible for stewarding a high-performance culture in which all Datavanters belong and thrive. We are proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, religion, national origin, disability, veteran status, or other legally protected status.

At Datavant our total rewards strategy powers a high-growth, high-performance, health technology company that rewards our employees for transforming health care through creating industry-defining data logistics products and services.

The range posted is for a given job title, which can include multiple levels. Individual rates for the same job title may differ based on their level, responsibilities, skills, and experience for a specific job.

The estimated total cash compensation range for this role is:

$104,000—$125,000 USD

To ensure the safety of patients and staff, many of our clients require post-offer health screenings and proof and/or completion of various vaccinations such as the flu shot, Tdap, COVID-19, etc. Any requests to be exempted from these requirements will be reviewed by Datavant Human Resources and determined on a case-by-case basis. Depending on the state in which you will be working, exemptions may be available on the basis of disability, medical contraindications to the vaccine or any of its components, pregnancy or pregnancy-related medical conditions, and/or religion.

This job is not eligible for employment sponsorship.

Datavant is committed to a work environment free from job discrimination. We are proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, religion, national origin, disability, veteran status, or other legally protected status. To learn more about our commitment, please review our EEO Commitment Statement here (https://www.datavant.com/eeo-commitment-statement) . Know Your Rights (https://www.eeoc.gov/know-your-rights-workplace-discrimination-illegal) , explore the resources available through the EEOC for more information regarding your legal rights and protections. In addition, Datavant does not and will not discharge or in any other manner discriminate against employees or applicants because they have inquired about, discussed, or disclosed their own pay.

If you choose to respond, your answers will be anonymous and will help us identify areas for improvement in our recruitment process. (We can only see aggregate responses, not individual ones. In fact, we aren’t even able to see whether you’ve responded.) Responding is entirely optional and will not affect your application or hiring process in any way.

If you need an accommodation while seeking employment, please request it here, (https://peopleteam.datavant.com/portal/en/newticket?departmentId=248697000248790029&layoutId=248697000248795462) by selecting the ‘Interview Accommodation Request’ category. Requests for reasonable accommodations will be reviewed on a case-by-case basis.

Crain’s Detroit Business named STG to Michigan’s Fastest Growing Companies list in both 2020 and 2019, Top IT Services Company’s List in 2020, 2019, 2018, and Top Minority Business Enterprise List in 2020, 2019, and 2018.

STG puts company CULTURE at the forefront of every business decision and employees are EMPOWERED and MEASURED for RESULTS. Both TEAMWORK and INDIVIDUAL Performance is recognized and rewarded.

From fulfilling a single patient’s request for their medical records to powering the AI revolution in healthcare, Datavanters are building the future of how data is connected and used to improve health.

By joining Datavant today, you’re stepping onto a driven and highly collaborative team that is passionate about creating transformative change in healthcare.

At Datavant, our mission is to protect, connect, and deliver the world’s health data to power better decisions and advance human health. As part of our internal team, you help us achieve this mission every day.

By continuing your career at Datavant, you’ll drive transformative change in healthcare alongside a collaborative team.

The Business Intelligence & Analytics function within Datavant Product is actively seeking a detail-oriented and impact-driven data analyst to strengthen our Corporate Cross-Functional Reporting team. This pillar focuses on building a scalable, trusted reporting platform that delivers consistent, transparent, and actionable insights across our finance, accounting, compliance, and people functions. In this role you will help mature our analytics portfolio, drive BI modernization, and enable operational visibility through data. If you are an analyst who thrives at the intersection of data and business, and enjoys collaborating with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, we would love to hear from you!

You Will:
• Partner & Analyze
• Partner with cross-functional stakeholders to understand business needs, scope requirements, and define analytical approaches that inform decision-making.
• Conduct exploratory and targeted analyses using analytical methods and business context to generate actionable recommendations.
• Design and develop business intelligence solutions, including dashboards, reports, and tools that support operational visibility and strategic insights.
• Deliver Scalable BI Solutions
• Lead the development and maintenance of reusable data models and visualizations, ensuring accuracy, scalability, and performance across internal and external reporting at Datavant.
• Collaborate with data engineering and BI teams to ensure data quality, accessibility, and alignment between source systems and reporting layers.
• Enable Continuous Improvement
• Contribute to the automation of recurring analytical workflows, reducing manual reporting and improving turnaround time for business users.
• Drive the growth of team practices and analytical maturity by mentoring peers, documenting solutions, and leading process improvements.
• Stay current on industry best practices in data visualization, BI tools, and analytics methods to continuously elevate team capabilities.

What You Will Bring to the Table:
• Technical Skills
• Proficiency in statistical analysis and programming languages such as SQL and Python for data analysis and modeling.
• Skill in designing clear, insightful visualizations tailored to varied audiences using tools such as Sigma, Power BI, or Tableau.
• Business Acumen & Communication
• Demonstrated ability to craft compelling, data-driven stories that inform strategy and influence decision-making.
• Excellent communication skills with the ability to distill technical concepts for non-technical audiences.
• Proven ability to manage time effectively and prioritize work to meet tight client deadlines.
• Mindset & Experience
• Exceptional initiative and ability to solve problems independently, seek help when needed, and take ownership when navigating ambiguity.
• Experience navigating change with cross-functional collaboration, customer focus, ownership, and a bias for action.
• 4+ years of relevant experience in data analytics or a related field.

Bonus Points If You Have:
• Experience working in healthcare, life sciences, or high-growth environments, especially in domains related to release of information (ROI) or data privacy compliance.
• Understanding of data privacy and security best practices, with awareness of key regulations (e.g., HIPAA).
• Experience working with cloud-based storage and computing platforms (e.g., AWS, GCP, Azure).
• Experience using dbt to build and maintain data models within a modern analytics stack.
• Proven ability to bring structure, best practices, and operational rigor to BI teams in high-growth or evolving data environments.
• Exposure to machine learning techniques such as natural language processing or time series forecasting.

job_description:
Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst - MMS job at CornerStone Technology Talent Services. Irving, TX.

Job Posting: Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst – MMS

CornerStone Technology Talent Services is seeking a Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst – MMS to support critical divestiture activities through accurate, timely, and actionable procurement data and analytics. This mid-level individual contributor role partners closely with sourcing, procurement, finance, and legal stakeholders to enable informed decision-making and track key milestones throughout the divestiture lifecycle.

Work Schedule & Location
• Schedule: Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM–5:00 PM (40 hours/week)
• Work Arrangement: Hybrid (in-office as needed)
• Location: Must reside in Irving, TX; Atlanta, GA; or Richmond, VA
• Assignment: Contract with potential extension based on business needs

Key Responsibilities
• Extract, validate, and deliver supplier and sourcing project data supporting divestiture activities
• Maintain procurement and sourcing data accuracy and consistency across systems
• Develop and support data governance standards related to divestiture efforts
• Design and maintain dashboards tracking procurement performance, milestones, and key metrics
• Identify and resolve data discrepancies between systems
• Support ERP and system updates tied to divestiture initiatives
• Provide ad-hoc reporting and analysis for cross-functional stakeholders
• Ensure data integrity, compliance, and adherence to internal controls
• Present insights and recommendations clearly to senior leaders

Required Skills & Experience
• 7+ years of experience in business intelligence, data analytics, or a similar analytical role
• Strong ability to interpret and translate complex data into actionable insights
• Advanced Excel skills (pivot tables, advanced formulas, data modeling)
• Proficiency with data visualization tools such as Power BI, Qlik, Tableau, or Looker
• Experience creating executive-level dashboards and reports
• Experience with process automation and identifying operational efficiencies
• Hands-on experience with ERP systems (SAP, Oracle, JDE, or similar), particularly procurement modules
• Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills
• Ability to collaborate cross-functionally and present to senior leadership
• Detail-oriented with a strong focus on data integrity and continuous improvement

Preferred Experience
• Background working with procurement, sourcing, or supply chain data
• Experience with Salesforce
• Comfort supporting divestiture-related or large-scale transformation initiatives

Location : 100 % Remote

Duration: 9 Months Contract

Experience: 7+ Years

Job Description

We are looking for a Senior BI Analytics Engineerwith strong expertise in Power BI, Snowflake, predictive analytics, and machine learning development. The ideal candidate will bridge business intelligence, advanced analytics, and data science to deliver actionable insights, scalable analytics solutions, and predictive models that drive business decision-making.

• * Experience with ETL/ELT concepts and analytics engineering workflows.
• * Familiarity with Git and CI/CD for analytics and ML solutions.
• * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.

job_description:
Position Overview

Ledgent Technology's client is actively seeking a Director of Business Intelligence & Marketing Analytics to join their team in California. This leadership role is crucial for driving the company's financial planning, forecasting, budgeting, pricing analysis, promotional performance measurement, and enterprise-wide data insights. The Director will oversee the Business Intelligence function, managing both the BI Manager and BI Analyst, ensuring high-quality data integrity, reporting, and analytics across all departments.

Responsibilities
• Partner with the Controller on budgeting, forecasting, long-range planning, and variance analysis across all service categories.
• Build and maintain financial models to support scenario planning, capital decisions, and growth strategies.
• Create and manage monthly reporting packages for leadership, including P&L performance, sales trends, labor forecasting, and profitability analysis.
• Own analysis of pricing effectiveness, elasticity, discount tests, multi-pet promotions, and regional rate strategies.
• Measure promotional performance (conversion, LTV impact, uplift, margin mix, CAC efficiency).
• Build frameworks to evaluate marketing campaign ROI, customer acquisition performance, retention behavior, and booking patterns.
• Translate customer and revenue insights into recommendations for Marketing, Operations, and Leadership.
• Develop forecasting models for seasonal demand, occupancy patterns, and promotional lift.
• Oversee BI strategy, reporting roadmap, dashboards, and analytics processes.
• Manage, mentor, and develop the Business Intelligence Manager and Business Intelligence Analyst.
• Ensure strong data governance, accuracy, standardization, and data hygiene across platforms.
• Partner with cross-functional teams to define KPI frameworks and improve visibility into performance.
• Evaluate and enhance reporting systems, financial tools, and BI solutions.
• Ensure integration and data flow between CRM, hotel management systems, analytics tools, and financial platforms.
• Work with vendors, IT, Marketing, and Operations to solve data issues and optimize systems.

Qualifications
• 7+ years in FP&A, business intelligence, marketing analytics, or related roles.
• Experience managing high-performing analytical teams.
• Proven track record supporting executive-level decision-making.
• Bachelor's degree required in Finance, Accounting, Economics, Business, Analytics, Statistics, Data Science, or a related field.
• Advanced Excel skills (financial modeling, Power Query, pivoting, forecasting).
• Strong SQL skills (data extraction, transformation, performance analysis).
• Experience with BI tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Zoho Analytics).
• Familiarity with financial planning & reporting systems.
• Experience with FP&A software such as Cube, Datarails, or Vena is preferred.
• Understanding of CRM systems, hotel management platforms, or POS/booking systems is preferred.
• Experience with GA4, Looker Studio, and marketing attribution tools is preferred.
• Strong financial acumen, analytical thinking, and attention to detail.
• Ability to simplify complex data and communicate insights clearly.
• Comfortable working across Finance, Marketing, Operations, HR, and Technology.
• Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
• Collaborative leadership style with excellent communication skills.
• Primarily sitting at a desk with occasional travel required.

Required Work Hours

Monday through Friday, standard business hours.

Benefits

The company offers a comprehensive benefits package, including health, dental, and vision insurance, retirement plans, and paid time off.

Additional Details

This is a supervisory position with a salary range of $130,000 to $145000 per year.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, age, ancestry, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, disability, medical condition, genetic information, pregnancy, or military or veteran status. We consider all qualified applicants, including those with criminal histories, in a manner consistent with state and local laws, including the California Fair Chance Act, City of Los Angeles' Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance, and Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance.

Description

This opportunity is for our Corporate CROSSMARK HQ, working \#hybrid on-site 3 days a week.

You will generate data and insights in support of category management and sales projects and initiatives (including category reviews, new item presentations, scorecards, assortment/shelving, and ad-hoc analytics).

Responsibilities

Essential Functions:
• Provide analytical support for category management and sales projects and initiatives utilizing various CPG and Retailer data sources and tools .
• Responsible for quality control of data sources and ensuring accuracy of deliverables , often requiring engagement with other BI associates and data/tool vendors.
• Build and update weekly and monthly dashboards and provide insights
• Participate in the development of sales presentations incorporating syndicated data and other sources of data that address business issues.
• Work closely with business managers to ensure quality results are delivered
• Keep abreast of clients KPIs, strategies, innovation, and other important information.

Qualifications

Education Requirements:
• Bachelor's Degree preferred

Work Experience Requirements:
• 1+ years of related work experience

Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities Requirements:
• Demonstrate an intermediate degree of proficiency in Windows and related software applications including Excel and PowerPoint. Knowledge of Power BI and advanced macros would be a strong asset.

job_description:
Are you ready to turn data into insights that drive better business decisions?Do you enjoy analyzing data, building dashboards, and partnering with teams to uncover meaningful trends and opportunities?

About the Business:

LexisNexis Risk Solutions is the essential partner in the assessment of risk. Within our Business Services vertical, we offer a multitude of solutions focused on helping businesses of all sizes drive higher revenue growth, maximize operational efficiencies, and improve customer experience. Our solutions help our customers solve difficult problems in the areas of Anti-Money Laundering/Counter Terrorist Financing, Identity Authentication & Verification, Fraud and Credit Risk mitigation and Customer Data Management. You can learn more about LexisNexis Risk at the link below, risk.lexisnexis.com

About Our Team:

You will work closely with Operations Program Management and crossfunctional partners in a collaborative, datadriven environment. Our team values curiosity, continuous learning, teamwork, and translating data into meaningful insights that support business outcomes while sharing best practices across the organization.

About the Role:

The BI Data Analyst II supports Operations Program Management and business stakeholders through data analysis, reporting, and dashboard development. This role focuses on building reliable, userfriendly dashboards and analyzing data to surface trends, insights, and opportunities. The BI Data Analyst II works collaboratively with senior analysts, reporting teams, and business partners to deliver accurate, actionable analytics that support informed decisionmaking.

This position can be remote or hybrid, with occasional onsite meetings as needed in Dayton, OH; Boca Raton, FL; or Alpharetta, GA.

Responsibilities:

Data Infrastructure & Modeling
• Understand underlying data structures and relationships across multiple datasets.
• Build and design SQL data models, views, and relationships to support reporting and analytics.
• Develop data models or repositories to transform raw data into structured, analysisready formats.
• Work with existing tools and reporting teams to improve raw data usability and consistency.

Dashboards & Reporting
• Develop clear, userfriendly Power BI dashboards with strong storylines that highlight actionable insights.
• Build dashboards that are scalable across teams and include drilldown capabilities.
• Proactively translate ideas, issues, and observations into visual analytics that drive business takeaways.
• Develop and manage automated data and report delivery routines based on agreed communication methods and timelines.

Analysis & Business Partnership
• Analyze data and dashboards to identify trends, anomalies, gaps, and areas of opportunity.
• Question inconsistencies and validate findings to ensure insights are accurate and reliable.
• Recommend costeffective solutions, followups, or next steps based on analytical findings.
• Partner with business stakeholders using a customerservice mindset to support decisionmaking.

Data Quality, Visualization & Governance
• Ensure data collected and reported is accurate, complete, and compliant with data standards.
• Test dashboards and technical solutions to ensure they function as expected prior to release.
• Create and maintain documentation related to data infrastructure, dashboards, and reports.
• Support ongoing improvements to reporting processes, standards, and best practices.
Requirements / Technical Skills:
• Experience with data collection and preparation, including data cleansing, measurement systems, relational databases, and SQL queries to join and transform complex data.
• Handson experience with exploratory data analysis to identify trends, patterns, and data anomalies or outliers.
• Strong data visualization skills, presenting actionable insights through clear, engaging dashboards and storytelling.
• Advanced knowledge of visualization tools, particularly Microsoft Power BI and Tableau.
• Advanced computer skills in Power BI, SQL, and Excel, with significant experience using the Microsoft Office Suite.
• Working knowledge of ETL tools such as KNIME or Alteryx is a plus.
• Strong problemsolving, analytical, and collaboration skills with a businesspartner and customerservice mindset.
• Resultsoriented with strong organizational skills and the ability to independently prioritize multiple initiatives.
• Professional communication skills, with the ability to explain data insights clearly to technical and nontechnical audiences.
Working for You:

We believe your wellbeing and success go hand in hand. That's why we offer a comprehensive and competitive benefits package, including:
• Health Benefits: Medical, dental, and vision coverage through a multicarrier program
• Retirement: 401(k) with company match and Employee Share Purchase Plan
• Wellbeing: Wellness platform with incentives, Headspace subscription, Employee Assistance Program, and generous timeoff policies
• Protection: Short and longterm disability, life and accidental death insurance, critical illness, and hospital indemnity plans
• Family Support: Paid parental bonding leave, family care leave, adoption, and surrogacy benefits
• Spending Accounts: Health Savings, Health Care, Dependent Care, and Commuter Spending Accounts
• Community & Inclusion: In addition to annual PTO, up to two paid days each year to participate in Employee Resource Groups and volunteer with a charity of your choice

U.S. National Base Pay Range: $59,200 - $98,600. Geographic differentials may apply in some locations to better reflect local market rates.

If performed in Ohio, the base pay range is $56,200 - $93,700.

This job is eligible for an annual incentive bonus.

We know your well-being and happiness are key to a long and successful career. We are delighted to offer country specific benefits. Click here to access benefits specific to your location.

We are committed to providing a fair and accessible hiring process. If you have a disability or other need that requires accommodation or adjustment, please let us know by completing our Applicant Request Support Formor please contact 1-855-833-5120.

Criminals may pose as recruiters asking for money or personal information. We never request money or banking details from job applicants. Learn more about spotting and avoiding scams here.

Please read our Candidate Privacy Policy.

We are an equal opportunity employer: qualified applicants are considered for and treated during employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, national origin, citizenship status, disability status, protected veteran status, age, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by law.

job_description:
Datavant is the data collaboration platform trusted for healthcare. Guided by our mission to make the world's health data secure, accessible and actionable, we provide critical data solutions for organizations across the healthcare ecosystem - including providers, health plans, researchers, and life sciences companies. From fulfilling a single patient's request for their medical records to powering the AI revolution in healthcare, Datavanters are building the future of how data is connected and used to improve health.

By joining Datavant today, you're stepping onto a driven and highly collaborative team that is passionate about creating transformative change in healthcare.

At Datavant, our mission is to protect, connect, and deliver the world's health data to power better decisions and advance human health. As part of our internal team, you help us achieve this mission every day.

By continuing your career at Datavant, you'll drive transformative change in healthcare alongside a collaborative team.

The Business Intelligence & Analytics function within Datavant Product is actively seeking a detail-oriented and impact-driven data analyst to strengthen our Corporate Cross-Functional Reporting team. This pillar focuses on building a scalable, trusted reporting platform that delivers consistent, transparent, and actionable insights across our finance, accounting, compliance, and people functions. In this role you will help mature our analytics portfolio, drive BI modernization, and enable operational visibility through data. If you are an analyst who thrives at the intersection of data and business, and enjoys collaborating with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, we would love to hear from you!
• *You Will:**

+ Partner & Analyze

+ Partner with cross-functional stakeholders to understand business needs, scope requirements, and define analytical approaches that inform decision-making.

+ Conduct exploratory and targeted analyses using analytical methods and business context to generate actionable recommendations.

+ Design and develop business intelligence solutions, including dashboards, reports, and tools that support operational visibility and strategic insights.

+ Deliver Scalable BI Solutions

+ Lead the development and maintenance of reusable data models and visualizations, ensuring accuracy, scalability, and performance across internal and external reporting at Datavant.

+ Collaborate with data engineering and BI teams to ensure data quality, accessibility, and alignment between source systems and reporting layers.

+ Enable Continuous Improvement

+ Contribute to the automation of recurring analytical workflows, reducing manual reporting and improving turnaround time for business users.

+ Drive the growth of team practices and analytical maturity by mentoring peers, documenting solutions, and leading process improvements.

+ Stay current on industry best practices in data visualization, BI tools, and analytics methods to continuously elevate team capabilities.
• *What You Will Bring to the Table:**

+ Technical Skills

+ Proficiency in statistical analysis and programming languages such as SQL and Python for data analysis and modeling.

+ Skill in designing clear, insightful visualizations tailored to varied audiences using tools such as Sigma, Power BI, or Tableau.

+ Business Acumen & Communication

+ Demonstrated ability to craft compelling, data-driven stories that inform strategy and influence decision-making.

+ Excellent communication skills with the ability to distill technical concepts for non-technical audiences.

+ Proven ability to manage time effectively and prioritize work to meet tight client deadlines.

+ Mindset & Experience

+ Exceptional initiative and ability to solve problems independently, seek help when needed, and take ownership when navigating ambiguity.

+ Experience navigating change with cross-functional collaboration, customer focus, ownership, and a bias for action.

+ 4+ years of relevant experience in data analytics or a related field.
• *Bonus Points If You Have:**

+ Experience working in healthcare, life sciences, or high-growth environments, especially in domains related to release of information (ROI) or data privacy compliance.

+ Understanding of data privacy and security best practices, with awareness of key regulations (e.g., HIPAA).

+ Experience working with cloud-based storage and computing platforms (e.g., AWS, GCP, Azure).

+ Experience using dbt to build and maintain data models within a modern analytics stack.

+ Proven ability to bring structure, best practices, and operational rigor to BI teams in high-growth or evolving data environments.

+ Exposure to machine learning techniques such as natural language processing or time series forecasting.

We are committed to building a diverse team of Datavanters who are all responsible for stewarding a high-performance culture in which all Datavanters belong and thrive. We are proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, religion, national origin, disability, veteran status, or other legally protected status.

At Datavant our total rewards strategy powers a high-growth, high-performance, health technology company that rewards our employees for transforming health care through creating industry-defining data logistics products and services.

The range posted is for a given job title, which can include multiple levels. Individual rates for the same job title may differ based on their level, responsibilities, skills, and experience for a specific job.

The estimated total cash compensation range for this role is:

$104,000-$125,000 USD

Depending on the state in which you will be working, exemptions may be available on the basis of disability, medical contraindications to the vaccine or any of its components, pregnancy or pregnancy-related medical conditions, and/or religion.

This job is not eligible for employment sponsorship.

In addition, Datavant does not and will not discharge or in any other manner discriminate against employees or applicants because they have inquired about, discussed, or disclosed their own pay.
